**E12-0: Governance rewrite + design lock land.** **As** the platform owner, **I want** `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` re-authored around E12 (Cosmic-era §4/§5/§6 archived as heritage; §1-3/§7-10 carried forward) plus this design doc committed, **so that** every downstream task has one authoritative rulebook for the egui/forked-desktop direction. **Acceptance:** AI_GOVERNANCE.md identity header reads MCNF 12.0 + names egui as the toolkit; §4 (egui/no-Carbon), §5 (forked desktop + spin), §6 (layered tiers) reflect the locks; the retired Carbon/libcosmic gates are removed from the doc's gate list; a heritage pointer to the Cosmic-era text exists. — DONE (2026-06-30, operator go-ahead): `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` rewritten to MCNF 12.0 "Quasar" (egui §4, forked-desktop §5, layered-tiers §6; §0-3/§7-10 carried forward; Cosmic-era text archived as heritage); design doc `docs/design/cosmic-magic-mesh-egui.md` landed. E12-1…E12-12 are the execution backlog.

**E12-1: `mde-egui` harness + shared `Style`/`Visuals` (the foundation, built FIRST).** **As** every surface author, **I want** one crate providing the eframe Wayland-client runner, the single shared `Style`/`Visuals` module, and the duration/easing table, **so that** all 6 surfaces share one toolkit + look with no per-surface styling drift. **Acceptance:** a trivial example client built on `mde-egui` launches as a Wayland client, renders a `Style`-themed window, and animates a `Style::DURATION`-driven `animate_bool`; the crate has zero libcosmic/iced deps; `Style` is the sole color/spacing source (no raw literals in the example). — DONE (2026-06-30): new `crates/shared/mde-egui` lands the three foundation pieces — `runner::run_client` (eframe wgpu/Wayland client runner that installs the shared look), `style::Style` (the single-source Quasar-dark palette + 8px spacing + type scale `Visuals`, applied via `Style::install`), `motion::Motion` (FAST/BASE/SLOW table wrapping egui `animate_bool`). a11y/accesskit deferred per lock 11 (eframe `accesskit` feature intentionally off — the absence IS the deferral). `examples/hello` is the acceptance surface (Style-themed window + Motion-timed reveal); zero libcosmic/iced deps; egui/eframe 0.31 pinned. Farm-green (slot egui1): example builds, 6 lib tests pass, clippy clean under `pedantic=warn`. Live Wayland launch is the hardware-gated `/preview` (optional per §7).

**E12-2: `mde-egui` DRM runner — egui owns the seat (no compositor).** **As** the shell author, **I want** a winit-less smithay **DRM/GBM + libinput** backend in `mde-egui` driving egui+wgpu on a GBM surface, **so that** the shell renders directly on the DRM/KMS seat with no Wayland compositor (replaces the retired cosmic-comp fork). **Acceptance:** a harness example runs on a bare VT/DRM seat (no compositor, no X), renders a `Style`-themed frame via wgpu-on-GBM, and processes real libinput keyboard/pointer events; degrades cleanly when no DRM master is available (CI/headless). — **DONE + FARM-GATED 2026-07-01 (BigBoy slot e12drm):** `crates/shared/mde-egui/src/drm.rs` (402 lines) is a complete 3-stage bare-seat backend — DRM/GBM primary-node bring-up (`open_primary_node`/`resolve_output`), EGL-on-GBM + `egui_glow` painter (the reliable bare-KMS GL path, not wgpu — see the module header), and the libinput seat + continuous page-flip present loop (Esc quits). The full native stack (`--features drm`: libdrm/gbm/khronos-egl/glow/egui_glow/input/udev) **compiles + links green** (39s cold on BigBoy) and the `headless_degrades_cleanly` test passes (9/9 lib tests) — `open_primary_node` returns the clean `DrmError::NoDrmMaster` fallback on a host with no seat, so the shell falls back to the windowed `run_client`. Both examples (`hello`, `hello_drm`) build+link under the feature. The **live-seat render + input round-trip on real DRM hardware is the hardware-gated `/preview`** (optional per §7 — same disposition as E12-1's live Wayland launch; a bare-KMS render can only be verified on a physical seat). E12-3's DRM-seat systemd boot rides this.

**E12-3: `mde-shell-egui` — the one shell on the DRM seat.** **As** every role, **I want** the single egui shell — a thin chrome bar (peers · sessions · status) that **expands into the full Workbench**, an egui **login/lock** (PAM + mesh identity), launched by **systemd onto the seat** at boot — **so that** the platform has one shell across headless/lighthouse/workstation. **Acceptance:** a Workstation boots straight to the egui shell on DRM (systemd unit, no display manager); the chrome bar lists live mesh peers + sessions and expands to the Workbench planes over the Bus; lock/unlock works against PAM; no compositor process exists. **Composition slice DONE (E12-3a+3b `9151686`):** the six surfaces compose into the one shell (chrome → Workbench + Fleet + Music/Files/Voice dock). Remaining for E12-3: DRM-seat systemd boot (rides E12-2's DRM runner) + PAM login/lock — hardware/PAM-gated. **Workbench planes:** Fleet live (MV-6); **ThisNode + Network now live too (WB-ThisNode `b94da19`, WB-Network `4b44b34`)** — both fold the world-readable `/run/mde/mesh-status.json` snapshot (§6-clean, no mackesd IPC): ThisNode shows role/overlay-IP/presence/heartbeat/per-daemon-health/peers/leader; Network shows the overlay (IP/CIDR/cipher/leader), mesh links (per-peer presence + lighthouse chips), network services (Nebula/DNS/Syncthing), and routing (gateways/routes/default-gw). Both honestly mark live telemetry (CPU/mem/disk · throughput/latency/handshake) not-published-to-this-surface rather than faking gauges. **Controller now live too (WB-Controller `e1c4133`)** — elected controller (leader + overlay-IP + tier), leader-lease/consensus signal, fleet-wide control-service rollup (mackesd/sync/bus); etcd raft/quorum telemetry + the in-process mackesd workers honestly marked/explained, not faked. **Provisioning now live too (WB-Provisioning `8768c4f`)** — per-node deployment tier + role rollup, fleet version-target vs each node's build (update posture), per-node enrollment readiness; live onboarding-wizard/install-step state honestly marked not-published-to-this-surface. **✅ ALL 4 snapshot planes now live (ThisNode · Controller · Network · Provisioning) + Fleet (MV-6) — the Workbench §7-sweep is COMPLETE: zero placeholder copy in any plane.** (Hand-merged with WB-Controller — both edit workbench.rs/main.rs; re-gated green, 79 shell tests.) *(RECONCILED [✓] 2026-07-02: the DRM-native shell is live — `mde-egui::run_drm` owns the seat, `mde-shell-egui` mounts every Surface, 168 tests green; the whole E12 session builds on it. Marker was drift.)*

**E12-3a-music: expose `music_panel(ui, &mut state)` from mde-music-egui** *(session=819545e6, a4c5a451)* — the surface's central view as an embeddable fn the shell calls (EMBED, `docs/design/e12-3-shell-composition.md`); its binary stays a thin frame wrapper. **DONE `49441cf`:** `pub fn music_panel(ui, &mut MusicApp)` (shell owns MusicApp + drains worker updates).

**E12-3b-mount: `mde-shell-egui` mounts Music/Files/Voice as in-shell panels** *(session=819545e6, 9151686)* — deps on E12-3a; a dock/launcher beside the Workbench (Datacenter/Fleet already mounted via MV-6). **DONE `9151686`:** new `dock.rs` (`Surface` enum + `rail()` launcher), `Nav`/`Shell` split — `Shell::new(cc)` builds all three surfaces once over their **real** backends (RealBackend/SIP/creds, no demo_data), `body()` drives each shown surface's `pump → header → push_id`-scoped `panel`. Surface libs grew the extraction seam: `music_pump`/`music_header`, `voice_pump`/re-exported `voice_header`, `files::real_browser()`. Farm-green on BigBoy (shell 32 tests incl. a headless mount-tessellate over the real backend; music 15/voice 11/files 18); clippy/fmt/layered-tiers 0. Re-gated green on the cherry-picked tree (`bdd8007`).

**E12-4: `mde-vdi` core + RDP (ironrdp → egui texture).** **As** a Workstation user, **I want** `mde-vdi-core` (session model) + `mde-vdi-rdp` decoding an RDP framebuffer into an **egui texture** with input forwarded, **so that** a VM desktop renders inside the shell with no external viewer. **Acceptance:** an RDP connection to a test guest renders live as a fullscreen egui texture in the shell, keyboard/pointer are forwarded, and the reserved escape chord returns to the chrome bar; no libcosmic/iced/external-viewer dependency. **STATUS (reconciled 2026-07-08):** `mde-vdi-rdp` crate + RDP-decode→egui-texture core is built, reachable from the shell Desktop surface (`vdi.rs`), and live-proven against the throwaway TESTVM RDP endpoint. Note: a separate `mde-vdi-core` crate does not exist; the session model lives inside `mde-vdi-rdp`.

title: **E12-5: Remote desktop over the mesh (FIRST end-to-end milestone).** *(session=819545e6, 145d377)* **As** a mesh user, **I want** to discover a remote VM (mesh service registry + DATACENTER inventory), connect **over Nebula** (RDP-primary), and drive it from the shell, with a `mackesd` **session-broker** worker tracking the session, **so that** I can use a desktop served by another peer. **Acceptance:** a VM desktop on a remote XCP-ng/Server peer is listed in the shell, connected over the Nebula overlay, rendered + interactive; the session-broker publishes the session; the mesh cert gates the connect (guest login still applies). **SHELL-RENDER SLICE DONE (E12-5a `145d377`):** the shell's new **Desktop surface** (`mde-shell-egui/src/vdi.rs`) mounts an rdp/vnc `Session` as an egui texture — `session.frame()→ColorImage→TextureHandle→paint`, egui input forwarded via `send_input`, Esc returns to chrome, honest "No desktop connected" EmptyState. This is the **first real caller** of `mde-vdi-rdp`/`mde-vdi-vnc` (dead-lib cleared). 36 shell tests green. **SESSION-BROKER DONE (E12-5b `07671f3`):** the `mackesd` `session_broker` worker — `VdiSession` + `SessionState` machine, `reconcile(desired,observed)`, leader-gated, reusing `NodeId`/`VmId` (no parallel model), `MeshSessionStore` gated with typed errors; spawned beside the scheduler (reachable). 20 tests. **DISCOVERY DONE (E12-5b-discovery `e1e2e17`):** the shell's remote-desktop picker (`mde-shell-egui/src/discovery.rs`, 450 lines) — lists mesh VMs from the reused Fleet inventory (a `read_inventory` extraction in datacenter.rs, no second source), and "Connect" emits a `session_broker::SessionRequest::Open` on `action/vdi/session` + hands the target to the Desktop surface's `VdiState`; honest "No remote desktops available" EmptyState. **REMAINING:** only the live Nebula connect to a remote guest (integration-gated) — the whole buildable E12-5 chain (discover → request → broker-track → render) is now wired end-to-end in-shell.

**E12-6: `mde-vdi-vnc` — VNC/XAPI-console fallback.** **As** a user, **I want** a `vnc-rs` path rendering the XCP-ng/QEMU VNC console into an egui texture, **so that** any guest (no RDP server, pre-boot, BIOS) is still reachable. **Acceptance:** a guest with no RDP renders + is interactive via VNC in the shell; the XAPI console location is resolved via the existing DATACENTER verb. **STATUS (reconciled 2026-06-30, session=819545e6):** `mde-vdi-vnc` crate + RFB-decode→egui-texture core BUILT + green (**63 tests**, farm-verified on .90). ✅ **NOW REACHABLE (E12-5a `145d377`):** the shell Desktop surface mounts a `VncSession` via the same `Session::Vnc(s) => s.frame()/s.send_input()` path (dead-lib cleared). **✅ LIVE-PROVEN 2026-07-01 (`c1b1831`):** a REAL XCP-ng guest console (QEMU, mcnf-build-51 on KVM-XCP1, resolved via the existing `vm-console` DATACENTER verb — no new resolver needed) rendered through the crate's public session path: RFB 3.8 handshake → 1024×768 ServerInit → Raw rect → `apply_rect` → egui ColorImage, and **input echoed** (`send_input` keystrokes changed the next framebuffer checksum, reproduced twice). Landed as the env-gated `live_console` test (`MDE_VNC_LIVE_TARGET=…`; XCP-ng consoles are dom0-local unix sockets — `/var/run/xen/vnc-<domid>`, NOT tcp 59xx — so the test needs a tunnel). **✅ LIVE SHELL-PROVEN 2026-07-09:** the in-shell `live-vdi` VNC worker connected to the KVM-XCP1 XAPI console socket via the same DATACENTER `vm-console`/domid path, rendered the live 1024×768 console frame, and input echoed through the shell worker (`fnv1a64 0xface601842022325 → 0x9ca76c76d58f3087`).

**E12-7: `mde-kvm` — local cloud-hypervisor VMs. — DROPPED / SUPERSEDED (reconciled 2026-07-10).** **As** a Workstation, **I want** to create/run local VMs on **cloud-hypervisor** with the **virtio-gpu zero-copy** fast path into the egui texture, each **dual-homed** (own Nebula cert + a LAN NIC), from **mesh golden images**, via a `mackesd` **vm-lifecycle** worker folded into the DATACENTER/Instances surface, **so that** local desktops run without a remote peer. **Acceptance:** a local Windows 10 golden-image VM boots on cloud-hypervisor, renders in the shell via virtio-gpu (dmabuf→wgpu), is dual-homed (its Nebula peer appears + it has a LAN IP), and is managed from the Instances panel; running disk lives in `~/Local`. — WAS IN PROGRESS before the QC-15 deletion (farm agent, salvaged + verified; kept below as historical record only): `crates/services/mde-kvm` broker core DONE — `VmSpec` + dual-homed `Nic` model, the pure `build_ch_config` (VmSpec→cloud-hypervisor VmConfig JSON), an injectable `ChTransport`/`UnixSocketTransport` (HTTP/1.1 over the CH api-socket), and a `Vm` create/boot/shutdown/info lifecycle. Kept separate from the Fleet plane (that's a Bus-fed libvirt projection; this is the node-local CH broker). **NO LONGER A LIVE REMAINDER (2026-07-10):** the local-VM-boot + virtio-gpu-render items once tracked here targeted cloud-hypervisor, which QC-15 has since deleted outright — this is not a task for a future drainer to resume on the old stack; see the QUASAR-CLOUD supersession note immediately below for where the functionality continues.

**BROKER-RESILIENCE-1: a skipped/unreachable broker is truly non-fatal.** Four-part fix: **(1)** the outbound-publish reqwest client now has a CONNECT (3 s) + overall REQUEST (5 s) timeout via a shared `hooks::publisher::broker_client()` used by both the webhook listener and the `mde-bus publish` CLI, so a publish to a dead broker fails FAST instead of hanging ~60 s. **(2)** when `broker::evaluate_prereqs` returns `Skip`, the daemon binds the webhook listener in a new persist-only mode (`ListenerConfig::persist_only_for_overlay_ip`) — it still ingests + persists + audits but skips the doomed outbound POST entirely (mirrors `--no-broker`), so a known-absent broker drives no outbound retries. **(3)** a conservative per-topic `/run` cap (`TOPIC_RUN_CAP_BYTES` = 8 MiB + 20k-entry backstop) now bounds EVERY non-audit topic to its most-recent window every retention pass (the analogue of the existing `audit/*` cap), so a down broker can never fill the tmpfs. **(4)** verified the sd_notify watchdog ping rides its own 250 ms timer in mackesd, sharing no task with any broker/spool call. **Acceptance:** a publish to a dead broker returns within the timeout (not ~60 s); a skipped-broker daemon persists without retrying outbound; the spool stays bounded under sustained no-broker publishes. *(build + clippy + new tests GREEN on the farm, slot `brokerfix`.)*

**BROKER-RESILIENCE-2 / WATCHDOG-2: the watchdog ping was STILL being starved — real root cause + fix (11.0.11, 2026-06-27).** BR-1 part (4)'s claim ("the ping rides its own 250 ms timer, sharing no task with any broker/spool call → can't be starved") was **false in practice**: both production lighthouses kept watchdog-aborting every ~90 s for ~40 h (lh1 1355 / lh2 1348 `status=6/ABRT`), across 11.0.8→11.0.10, which blocked every enrollment (`:4243` only up between crashes). The ping rode `tokio::time::sleep` **on the runtime**, and on a **1-vCPU droplet** `new_multi_thread` defaults to a SINGLE worker that also owns the **tokio time driver**; when that worker reaches a blocking bridge (`substrate::peers::block_on` → `block_in_place` for an etcd round-trip) the time driver **freezes**, the sleep never fires, and systemd SIGABRTs a healthy daemon. A missing broker reliably triggered it via extra `block_in_place` churn. Fix: **(a)** floor the runtime at 4 worker threads even on 1 vCPU (`bin/mackesd.rs`), so a second worker keeps timers alive while another blocks; **(b)** move the heartbeat to a **dedicated OS thread** gated on an async liveness beacon the serve loop stamps every 250 ms (`sd_notify::watchdog_should_ping`) — kernel-scheduled (unstarvable) yet still reflects true liveness (a genuine wedge stops the beat → ping withheld → systemd restarts). Released as signed **11.0.11** (RSA-4096 d0921c73 verifies on F43) → GitHub Release + gh-pages dnf channel (fedora-43/44). **Acceptance:** lh1/lh2 stop crash-looping after upgrade (NRestarts stops climbing, zero new ABRT, `:4243` stays up) — the live deploy/verify is the remaining step (operator-gated channel `dnf upgrade`). *(farm: 7/7 lib tests incl. new beacon tests, clippy+fmt clean, `cargo check --bin` exit 0.)*

**BROKER-RESILIENCE-3: the broker is now turn-key PRESENT on a fresh lighthouse, not just non-fatal when absent.** BR-1/2 made a missing `ntfy` broker survivable; this makes it actually get installed at provision time. The chain was already wired (RPM vendors the pinned `ntfy_2.24.0` tarball at `/usr/share/magic-mesh/vendor/`; `mesh-install-ntfy` is shipped + bundled-first/offline-capable + idempotent; post_install `enable`s `mesh-broker-setup.service`), but the oneshot is only `enable`d — NOT `--now` (deliberately, so the dnf transaction never blocks on `network-online.target`). So on a freshly provisioned lighthouse that never reboots, there was no broker until the first reboot. Fix: both lighthouse cloud-inits (`do-lighthouse-cloudinit.sh` found-path + `do-lighthouse-join-cloudinit.sh` join-path) now `systemctl start --no-block` the three first-boot fetch oneshots (`mesh-broker-setup` / `mesh-netdata-setup` / `mesh-shell-setup`) after the daemon comes up — post-boot, network up, idempotent, non-stalling. **Acceptance:** a just-provisioned lighthouse has `/usr/bin/ntfy` (and netdata + starship) without a reboot; re-running is a no-op. *(`bash -n` clean on both scripts; pure cloud-init template change, no compile.)*

**FA-1: tofu shape model.** **As** the farm, **I want** a per-dom0 `shape` var (`big`/`small`/`off`) + `small_count` driving `infra/tofu/build-vms.tf`, **so that** `tofu apply` provisions one hardware-maxing big VM, N standard small VMs, or nothing. **Acceptance:** `shape=big` on BigBoy provisions ~10vCPU/26GiB `mcnf-build-big-52`; `shape=small,count=2` provisions 2 standard VMs; `shape=off` destroys them (scale-to-zero); idempotent re-apply.

**FA-2: golden `clean` snapshot + fast reset.** **As** the autoscaler, **I want** each toolchained build VM to carry a post-toolchain `clean` snapshot and a `farm-vm reset <vm>` that `xe snapshot-revert`s it, **so that** inter-job state returns to baseline without a full re-clone. **Acceptance:** a build dirties the VM, `reset` reverts to clean (toolchain + sccache priming intact, job state gone), verified on a reset drill.

**FA-3: the farm-autoscaler reconcile step (no AI).** **As** the FARM-AUTO reconciler, **I want** a step that reads the `@farm` queue, classifies each pending job big/small, computes the desired per-dom0 shape, writes the tofu vars, and `tofu apply`s — scaling to zero when idle, **so that** the farm matches demand with zero AI tokens. **Acceptance:** a queued whole-workspace build → big VM provisioned + the build runs + scaled down after; many per-crate jobs → small×N provisioned + run in parallel + scaled to zero; no AI tokens spent.

**FA-4: mutual-exclusion drain/preempt + hysteresis.** **As** a dom0, **I want** to run EITHER big OR small (never both), switching only after draining current jobs, with a min-dwell debounce, **so that** shape changes don't thrash against tofu's ~minutes apply latency. **Acceptance:** a big job queued while smalls run → smalls drain, then destroy+provision big; a dom0 never hosts big+small simultaneously; no flip within the dwell window.

**FA-5: POD-level scaling.** **As** the autoscaler, **I want** to cap agent/build podman pods per VM to its vCPU/RAM budget and fold pod demand into the shape choice, **so that** many queued agent pods provision small×N rather than starving one big VM. **Acceptance:** pod count per VM never exceeds its core budget; a pod-heavy queue biases the dom0 toward the small shape.

**FA-6: route builds/agents by shape.** **As** a build job, **I want** `xcp-build.sh` + the drain coordinator to target the autoscaler-provisioned VM matching my shape (big→BigBoy's big VM, small→the small pool), **so that** jobs land on the right elastic resource. **Acceptance:** a whole-workspace build routes to the big VM; per-crate builds spread across the small pool; routing degrades to BigBoy if the autoscaler is paused.

**FA-7: lifecycle observability.** **As** an operator, **I want** the autoscaler to log every create/destroy + publish the live farm topology (per-dom0 shape + utilization) to a CLI/Workbench surface, **so that** on-demand use is auditable. **Acceptance:** each scale event is logged with the reason; `farm topology` (CLI + panel) shows current shapes, running VMs, and per-dom0 utilization.

**EFF-1 · SEC · FileXfer Send-To path traversal (target).** `ipc/files.rs:265` joins the `peer:<target>` selector raw into `inbox_root(target)` — `peer:../../etc` escapes the QNM root (`create_dir_all` + write anywhere mackesd can). Reject `target` with `/`,`..`,`\0`,non-`[A-Za-z0-9._-]`; assert the resolved dest is under `<qnm>/inbox/`. *(Regression from AUD-1 this session.)*

**EFF-2 · SEC · FileXfer Send-To arbitrary-file read (sources).** `ipc/files.rs:290` copies caller-supplied absolute `sources` verbatim → a Bus writer can exfil `/etc/shadow`/keys into a peer's replicated inbox. Confine sources to an allowlisted root (operator home / declared share); refuse symlinks + paths outside it. — DONE: `FileXfer` gained a `share_root` (defaults to the operator home, canonicalized; `with_share_root` override). `allowed_source()` canonicalizes each source (resolving symlinks + `..`), requires a regular file, and refuses anything not `starts_with(share_root)` — refused sources are skipped + warn-logged + counted in the reply's `refused` field. Two regression tests: a source outside the root is refused while an in-root one delivers, and a symlink-under-root → outside is refused.

**EFF-3 · SEC · FileXfer rollback path traversal.** `ipc/files.rs:338` removes files from `inbox_root(target).join(host).join(f)` off forgeable outbox rows. Same target/basename validation + canonicalize-and-contain before `remove_file`.

**EFF-4 · REL · Supervisor doesn't restart on worker PANIC.** `workers/mod.rs:481` matches only `Ok/Err`; a panicking `run()` becomes a logged `JoinError`, never restarted. Wrap each `worker.run()` so a panic → restartable `Err` (feed the back-off/breaker).

**MESH-1 · DESIGN/SEC · enrollment has no network bootstrap — remote/NAT'd peers can't self-join.** `nebula_enroll` is *QNM-Shared-mediated only*: the peer writes `pending-enroll.json` to the shared volume, the lighthouse auto-signer writes the bundle back (error path literally says "Check QNM-Shared is mounted + writable"). A fresh peer must therefore *already* share QNM-Shared (LizardFS/NFS) with the lighthouse — but QNM-Shared rides the overlay, and you can't reach the overlay until enrolled → chicken-and-egg with no built-in resolver. The join token carries the lighthouse's public addr but enroll never uses it for the *signing handshake* (only for the later `static_host_map`). bed-mesh "worked" only because the co-located cloud pair NFS-shared QNM-Shared out-of-band; the LAN peers needed a `fuse-sshfs` bridge hack. **FINISH:** add a network enrollment path — lighthouse exposes a CSR-sign endpoint on its public IP (reuse the nebula-https-tunnel :443 / bearer-authed) so a remote peer submits its CSR + gets the signed bundle over the network, *then* mounts QNM-Shared over the overlay for steady state. The shared-FS path is fine for co-located nodes; it cannot be the only path for a public-lighthouse mesh.

**MESH-2 · BUG · peer materialized the lighthouse `static_host_map` with a LOCAL interface IP.** On the `fedora` peer, `nebula_supervisor` rendered `/etc/nebula/config.yaml` as `"10.42.0.1": ["192.168.122.1:4242"]` — `192.168.122.1` is the peer's own libvirt `virbr0`, not the lighthouse's public `45.55.33.179`. The received bundle was CORRECT (`external_addr: 45.55.33.179:4242`); the render clobbered it with a locally-detected address → nebula dials a dead end, overlay never forms (100% loss). The co-located cloud peer `.51` rendered correctly (it has no libvirt/global-scope local IP to mis-pick). **FINISH:** root-cause the static_host_map render — the lighthouse entry must use `bundle.lighthouses[].external_addr`, never a locally-detected interface address; add a render test pinning a non-self lighthouse entry to the bundle's external_addr even when the local host has a global-scope private IP (libvirt/docker bridges).

**ONBOARD-1: join token v3 — embed the endpoint cert fingerprint + enroll port.** Done — `nebula_enroll.rs` `JoinToken` gained `fp: Option<String>` (`#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if)]`, additive); wire form `mesh:<id>@<ip>:<port>#<bearer>?fp=<sha256-hex>`; `parse_join_token` splits `?fp=` off the bearer + validates 64-char lowercase hex; `encode()` round-trips the suffix; `JOIN_TOKEN_MAX_LEN` 120→200 (fits the +68-char fp, still QR-friendly). v2.5 tokens parse with `fp: None`. 36/0 lib tests (added `parse_round_trips_a_token_with_fp` + `parse_rejects_malformed_fp`). *(Mint-side fp injection lands with the endpoint in ONBOARD-2/4 — the fp is the endpoint cert's, which doesn't exist until `found`.)*

**ONBOARD-2: lighthouse rustls `/enroll` endpoint.** Done. New `nebula_enroll_endpoint` module: self-signed endpoint identity (`generate_endpoint_identity` → cert/key PEM + SHA-256 `fingerprint` of the DER, the value tokens pin), minimal HTTP/1.1 framing (`parse_request`, 64 KiB body cap), and the pure `handle_enroll` (parses a `PendingEnrollment`, signs via the new shared `sign_csr_into_bundle` core, redeems the single-use bearer, returns the bundle JSON; maps SignCsrError → 400/401/403/409/500). New `workers::nebula_enroll_listener` rustls worker (default `:4243`, ring provider, per-conn timeout, spawn_blocking signing) wired into `run_serve` — spawns only when the endpoint cert is present (same gate as the covert :443 listener; peer-role boxes skip), roster reuses the csr-watcher external-addr probe so bundles advertise the PUBLIC addr (MESH-2 guard). `sign_pending_csr` refactored to delegate to the shared core (file flow unchanged, 13/13 sign tests green). **Real end-to-end TLS test:** a fp-pinning rustls client POSTs a CSR → 200 + bundle → bearer spent. 54/0 nebula_enroll tests. *(`found`-time cert generation + token-fp injection wire up in ONBOARD-4; the cert-gen + fp primitives they need are built here.)*

**ONBOARD-3: peer network-enroll client (fingerprint-pinned).** Done — new `nebula_enroll_client` module. `PinnedCertVerifier` (rustls `ServerCertVerifier`): accepts ONLY the cert whose SHA-256 == token fp AND verifies the TLS handshake signature via the ring provider (so a MITM can neither swap the cert nor replay the public cert without its key — fail-closed). `enroll_over_network` opens a fp-pinned TLS conn, POSTs the CSR to `/enroll`, parses status+bundle (maps non-200 → `Refused{status,message}`). `network_enroll` builds identity+CSR from the token, enrolls, and `persist_bundle`s — materializes `/etc/nebula` via the supervisor's `materialize_config` (`static_host_map` from `external_addr`, MESH-2 guard) + drops the bundle at QNM-Shared `bundle_path` for steady-state. **Full-stack tests** (real pinned client ↔ real `serve_connection`): happy path → bundle + bearer spent + config.yaml carries the PUBLIC `203.0.113.7:4242`; **fp-mismatch → fail-closed, bearer NOT spent**. 60/0 nebula_enroll tests. *(`serve`→overlay dial is exercised live in ONBOARD-8; the materialized config that drives it is verified here.)*

**ONBOARD-4: `found` + `join` verbs.** Done — two new CLI verbs in `bin/mackesd.rs`. `mackesd found <mesh-id> [--external-addr auto] [--role] [--enroll-port]`: detects the primary outbound IPv4 (UDP-connect trick; behind-NAT operators pass it explicitly), generates + persists the `/enroll` endpoint identity (`/etc/nebula/enroll-endpoint.{crt,key}`, key 0600), runs `mesh_init` (role pin + CA + self-sign + bundle + bearer), then re-expresses the minted bearer as a **v3 token** pinning the endpoint fp + enroll port pointing at the public IP, and prints a ready-to-paste `mackesd join '<token>'` line. `mackesd join <token> [--role] [--name]`: parses the token, pins the role (if unpinned), and — when the token carries `fp` — runs the fingerprint-pinned `network_enroll` (ONBOARD-3) materializing `/etc/nebula`; fp-absent tokens fall back to the legacy QNM-Shared `enroll_with_token`; no token → launches the `mde-enroll` TUI (ONBOARD-5) with a clear fallback message. The `nebula-enroll-listener` activates on the next `serve` (the cert now exists). Smoked: `found --help`, `join` bad-token → "invalid join token", `join` no-token → TUI-fallback guidance. The found→join token/enroll path is the exact shape ONBOARD-3's full-stack test exercises end-to-end. 60/0 enroll tests, clean clippy.

**OBS-9: mde-kdc-host RSA-4096 keygen tests appeared to hang the workspace gate** — ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED 2026-06-11. `keygen::generate_pkcs8` (RSA-4096, the locked Q23 identity) runs through the pure-Rust `rsa` + `num-bigint-dig` crates; **unoptimized in a debug build, a single keygen takes minutes**, so `keygen::tests::generate_pkcs8_returns_loadable_keypair` and `first_pair::tests::first_pair_captures_…` (which keygens in `spawn_device`) blew past any reasonable timeout and stalled `cargo test --workspace`. NOT a deadlock — the same keygen test passes in **3.84 s** under the release profile. Fix: `[profile.dev.package.rsa]` + `[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]` `opt-level = 3` in the root `Cargo.toml` — debug keygen drops from >120 s to ~4 s, RSA-4096 unchanged, rest of the workspace still compiles fast. (A misdiagnosed `#[ignore]` was added then removed.)

**AUDIT-MESH-3: `mesh-fs-status` reports `peers:[] goal:0`.** Mesh Storage shows master reachable but no chunkservers/goal/usage. **Root cause:** the whole meshfs admin layer shelled MooseFS's `mfsadmin <vip> CS-LIST`, but the deployed substrate is **LizardFS 3.12**, whose admin CLI is `lizardfs-admin` (and the `lizardfs-adm` package wasn't even installed) — so `master_reachable` (TCP probe) succeeded but the CS query always failed → empty peers. **Fixed:** new `query_chunkservers()` shells `lizardfs-admin list-chunkservers <vip> 9421 --porcelain` + `parse_chunkservers_porcelain()` (avail = total−used, `:port` stripped); all read-paths (status panel, quota tick `min_chunkserver_avail_bytes`, reconcile `current_chunkserver_ips`, CA-backup `run_cs_list`) funnel through it; `DEFAULT_ADMIN_BINARY` + the `mesh-fs-status` CLI default are now `lizardfs-admin`. Parser tested against the live 2-CS porcelain fixture. **Substrate:** `lizardfs-adm` installed on Lighthouse-02 (provides `lizardfs-admin`); must become an RPM `Requires:` (folded into [AUDIT-MESH-6 dep work] + AUDIT-MESH-7) and installed on every chunkserver node. **Acceptance:** mesh-fs-status carries chunkserver peers + goal + usage; Mesh Storage renders them. *(mackesd code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*

**AUDIT-MESH-7: meshfs write-path admin commands used dead MooseFS `mfsadmin` syntax. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); CLOSED 2026-06-21.** *(Real lizardfs-admin argv reachable in meshfs_worker — Phase-0 rescue GREEN; live-verify de-gated per operator 2026-06-21. NB: this code is retired wholesale by SUBSTRATE-6 when LizardFS is removed.)* Mapped each to its real LizardFS behavior: **evict** (`CS-EVICT`) → removed; LizardFS auto-replicates a departed chunkserver's chunks, so the worker now just records the departure + logs the replication backlog via the real `lizardfs-admin chunks-health <vip> <port> --replication --porcelain` (`chunks_health_argv`). **failover** (`MASTER-STOP`) → removed; real VIP failover is the existing `tick_once_ha` (claim VIP + promote shadow), not a remote master-stop. **topology** (`CS-SET-TOPOLOGY`) → removed; LizardFS topology is file-side (`/etc/mfs/mfstopology.cfg` + `reload-config`), and a flat ≤8-peer mesh needs no per-CS labels. **undelete** (`TRASH-RECOVER`) → reimplemented as the real LizardFS mechanism: `rename` the trash entry into the trash tree's `undel/` inbox (`dispatch_undelete`). Removed the dead `evict_argv`/`failover_vip_argv`/`set_topology_argv`/`master_reachable_via_shadow`; dropped the unused `admin_binary` dispatcher param. Tests: `chunks_health_argv_is_real_lizardfs_syntax`, `dispatch_undelete_moves_entry_into_undel`, `dispatch_undelete_missing_entry_reports_error` (+ updated existing). **RPM dep:** `lizardfs-adm` (provides `lizardfs-admin`) can't be a hard `Requires:` (absent from F44 base repos) — it's provisioned by **BIRTHRIGHT-1** (bundled fc43 RPMs, installed for lighthouse/server roles). **Live-verify (before close):** on the mesh, a peer departure logs the replication backlog; undelete restores a trashed file; no command emits MooseFS syntax. *(was: dead non-functional MooseFS argv.)*

Tests: `get_state_is_answered_without_engine_or_creds`. **Acceptance:** get-state always returns a parseable state; Music panel shows playing/idle/needs-config honestly. *(musicd + workbench code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*

**AUDIT-MESH-13: ~~cross-UID SQLite bus index invisibility~~ — DISPROVEN / MISDIAGNOSIS (corrected 2026-06-15).** I initially concluded all `action/*` RPCs fail because the SQLite `messages` table isn't visible cross-uid. **That was wrong — a test artifact.** Re-verified rigorously: `sqlite3` as root reads mm's just-written rows fine; a **long-lived** Python connection sees another process's new write (0→1); root `mde-bus history` **with `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` actually set** sees mm's rows. The persist layer is fully cross-uid + cross-connection correct. **The real artifact:** my CLI tests ran in a **non-login ssh shell that has no `MDE_BUS_ROOT`**, so the CLI resolved to the **per-HOME** bus (`~/.local/share/mde/bus`) — a different spool from `/run/mde-bus` where the responders listen → "no responder." The GUI (which *does* carry `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` via env.d) uses the shared root. **Proof the RPC infra works:** with `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` set, `action/nebula/self-node` → `{node_id:…}` ✓ and `action/connect/devices` → `[{…moto g…}]` ✓ both reply. No cross-uid bug exists; no persist-layer change needed. *(Lesson: when testing the bus by CLI, export `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` to match the GUI, or the per-HOME fallback masquerades as a dead responder.)*

**AUDIT-MESH-16 (CRITICAL — the REAL cross-uid bus bug; root cause of music "no data" + any non-root responder): shared-spool subdirectories aren't cross-uid writable, so a desktop-user responder's replies are silently dropped.** Definitively root-caused (2026-06-15): `action/music/*` verbs all timed out because **mde-musicd runs as the desktop user (mm)** and its reply write to `/run/mde-bus/reply/<ulid>` failed — `/run/mde-bus/reply/` is `drwxr-xr-x root root` (created by the root mackesd daemon at umask 022), so mm got EPERM, and `poll_transport` ignores write errors (`let _ = persist.write`). The spool ROOT is sticky-1777 (any uid can make top-level dirs) but `Persist::write_full` only `relax_shared`'d the **leaf** topic dir, never the intermediate parent `create_dir_all` made — so `reply/` stayed 0755. mackesd's own responders (root) were unaffected → nebula/connect worked, music didn't. **Fixed:** `persist.rs::relax_topic_chain` now relaxes EVERY path component (parent + leaf) to 0777 on each write, so any mesh uid can write replies. **Verified live:** after a stop-gap `chmod 1777` of the spool parents on .13, get-state replies instantly `{ok:true,active:false,audio_available:true,needs_airsonic:false,…}`. **Acceptance:** a desktop-user responder's replies reach the requester; verified get-state on .13 ✓. *(mde-bus lib change → rebuilds mackesd+musicd+mde-bus; live-patched both Workstations via chmod; durable via the next RPM roll.)*

**AUDIT-MESH-14: `action/music/get-state` stall — RESOLVED (the blocker was AUDIT-MESH-16's spool perms).** Two real hardening fixes shipped along the way and kept: **#1** Airsonic `reqwest::Client` now has 3s connect + 10s total timeouts (`airsonic.rs::build_http_client`) so a dead Airsonic can't hang `poll_browse` forever; **#2** `bus_responder::serve` reordered so the fast local responders (`poll_once`/`poll_transport`/`poll_peers`) run before the network-bound `poll_browse`. Both are correct defense-in-depth, but the actual cause of the timeout was AUDIT-MESH-16 (musicd as mm couldn't write into the root-owned `reply/`). **Verified:** get-state replies on .13 with the new musicd + the spool chmod. *(musicd deployed to host + .13.)*

**AUDIT-MESH-15 (operator-reported, 2026-06-15): `~/Documents` does NOT auto-sync across the mesh — the FPG-7 XDG bind-mount is broken on two counts. FIXED (2026-06-16).** Both bugs fixed in `meshfs_worker.rs`: (1) **wrong home** — new `desktop_user_homes()` parses `/etc/passwd` for the real uid-1000..60000 desktop user(s) under `/home`; the tick now binds for each of them (with a headless fall-back to the daemon's `$HOME`), so it binds `/home/<u>/Documents` not the daemon's `/root/Documents`. **BULLETPROOF-1: the bus retention GC never ran on deployed nodes, and its quota exceeded the tmpfs — so the `audit/*` lane filled `/run` and bricked both lighthouses.** Live (2026-06-16): LH-01 + LH-02 `/run` (190 MB tmpfs) at 100%, 84 MB of unreaped `audit/*` (retention=forever). **Two root causes:** (1) the GC pass (`mde_bus::retention::run_pass_at`) only ran inside the standalone `mde-bus` daemon, but mackesd embeds the bus as a library and ships **no `mde-bus.service`** → retention NEVER ran on any node; (2) the quota (soft 500 MB / hard 2 GB) was larger than a 190 MB `/run`, so eviction could never fire before ENOSPC. **Fix:** (a) mackesd now spawns a `bus-retention-gc` thread (`run_serve`) running a pass every 120 s; (b) the quota default is now tmpfs-safe (96/144 MB) and mackesd sets a **filesystem-relative** cap (~50% hard / ~33% soft of the hosting fs via `df`); (c) `run_pass_at` gained a **hard-cap eviction safety valve** — when over the hard cap it sheds oldest-first, non-`audit/*` first and `audit/*` only as a last resort (a full `/run` downs the whole node, strictly worse than losing ephemeral tmpfs audit that doesn't survive reboot — a documented, bounded exception to the Q28 audit-forever lock). Tests: `hard_cap_evicts_oldest_first_until_under_soft`, `hard_cap_sheds_non_audit_before_audit`, `under_hard_cap_evicts_nothing`, `default_quota_is_tmpfs_safe` (mde-bus 334 green). **Acceptance:** on every node the bus stays bounded well under its `/run`; `journalctl -u mackesd` shows periodic "bus retention pass"; a flooded `audit/*` lane is shed before ENOSPC. *(live remediation already applied: both lighthouses' `/run` freed + mackesd restarted; the durable fix deploys with the RPM roll.)*

**BULLETPROOF-2: systemd watchdog (`WatchdogSec` + `sd_notify`) so a hung mackesd auto-restarts.** A wedged responder thread (e.g. D-state on a bad FUSE read) is invisible to systemd's process-alive check. **Done:** new dependency-free `mackesd_core::sd_notify` (raw `$NOTIFY_SOCKET` datagram; filesystem-path form, graceful no-op for abstract/non-systemd); `run_serve` sends `READY=1` once the supervisor + responders are up and pings `WATCHDOG=1` from the main watch loop (a wedged runtime stops pinging → restart); `mackesd.service` is now `Type=notify` + `NotifyAccess=main` + `WatchdogSec=60` + `TimeoutStartSec=120`. Tests: 5 in `sd_notify` (incl. real-socket round-trip + watchdog-interval arming). **LIVE-VERIFIED on fedora (2026-06-16):** under `Type=notify` mackesd reached `active` via `READY=1`, armed the watchdog (`secs:30`), held a full cycle with `NRestarts=0`, healthz kept replying. (Committed `46f5c11`→ see also the notify commit.) **Fleet deploy pending the RPM roll + BULLETPROOF-4.**

**BULLETPROOF-4: `/etc/systemd/system/mackesd.service` shadow — SWEPT FLEET-WIDE (2026-06-17).** Audited all 9 nodes: only the dev host (`fedora`) carried the `/etc` shadow → removed (now `FragmentPath=/usr/lib/systemd/system/mackesd.service`, mackesd active); the other 8 (.13, KVM1, MDE-VM-1..4, both lighthouses) were already clean. **BULLETPROOF-4 (original): a manual `/etc/systemd/system/mackesd.service` shadows the packaged `/usr/lib` unit → RPM unit updates never apply.** Found live verifying BP-2 on fedora: the effective `FragmentPath` was `/etc/systemd/system/mackesd.service` (created out-of-band, not by any repo script — only `.service.d/` drop-ins are repo-managed), so the BP-2 `Type=notify` change in the packaged `/usr/lib` unit was masked until I installed to `/etc`. **Fix:** the roll (or the RPM `%post`) must remove a full `/etc/systemd/system/mackesd.service` shadow (keeping `.service.d/` drop-ins) so the packaged unit governs; audit all 4 nodes for the shadow. *(mildly destructive — removing an admin `/etc` file — so operator-reviewed at roll, not a blind scriptlet.)* **Acceptance:** on every node `systemctl show mackesd -p FragmentPath` resolves to `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mackesd.service`; an RPM unit change takes effect on upgrade. _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by the [✓] sibling above (~877); 16d7b2a fleet-wide /etc shadow sweep, 9 nodes FragmentPath=/usr/lib)_

**BULLETPROOF-3: `systemd-tmpfiles` age/size guard on `/run/mde-bus`** — **CLOSED as superseded (2026-06-19), intentionally NOT implemented as specified.** The acceptance requires bounding the spool "without creating index/file divergence", but `systemd-tmpfiles` can only age- or boot-delete files — exactly the blind deletion that strands `index.sqlite` rows ([[BUS-INODE-ORPHAN]]); it cannot do size-bounded, index-coordinated eviction. The divergence-free bound is **in-process** and now complete: **BULLETPROOF-1** (the GC reaper + hard-cap eviction, mackesd is the sole writer so the spool can't grow while it's down), **BUS-RETENTION-1** (counts + VACUUM-reclaims `index.sqlite` so the DB can't fill `/run`), and **BUS-RETENTION-2** (a `/run`<15% headroom alert + a parallel `mesh-health-check.sh` guard that runs even if mackesd is wedged). A tmpfiles rule would add risk (divergence) with no coverage those three don't already provide, so it is deliberately omitted.

Daemon log: `decode_track failed: error decoding response body` + audio underrun. `decode_track` did `reqwest::blocking::get(url).bytes()` (buffers the whole body before decoding) — fine for a finite song (Content-Length present) but it never returns for an infinite icecast/shoutcast stream. **Fix:** branch on Content-Length — finite tracks stay buffered into a seekable Cursor; a stream with no Content-Length is piped through a producer thread into an unseekable `ReadOnlySource` (`std::io::pipe` PipeReader is Send+Sync) so symphonia decodes it continuously. *(committed `MUSIC-RADIO`; deployed live to .13; ships durably in the next roll — NOT in the published v10.0.11.)*

**MUSIC-BROWSE/ART: "Browse is broken in library" + "No Artwork Appears" — almost certainly the daemon-not-running symptom (now fixed), re-verify.** The Airsonic endpoints all return `ok` at the negotiated v1.15.0 (getArtists/getAlbumList2/getInternetRadioStations tested directly), and the client chain IS wired: `parse_items` populates `LibraryItem.art_id` from `coverArt`, the GUI's `ItemsLoaded` handler fans out per-card art fetches → `ArtLoaded` → `art_cache`, and the daemon serves `get-cover-art` (base64 over the bus). So browse+art failed because `mde-musicd` wasn't running on the GUI's bus (MUSIC-DAEMON-AUTOSTART) — now started on .13. **Re-verify on .13 after reopening the Music app:** browse navigates + grid art renders. If art still fails with the daemon up, suspect a bus message-size cap on the base64 cover bytes (chunk or serve a cover URL/file instead) — investigate then. **Acceptance:** library browse navigates and album/artist cards show artwork on a Workstation with the daemon running.

**MUSIC-DAEMON-AUTOSTART: `mde-musicd` never auto-ran on a Workstation → "daemon not responding" — FIXED.** The unit already shipped as a USER service (`/usr/lib/systemd/user/mde-musicd.service`) but was never enabled. Fix: the RPM `%post` now `systemctl --global enable mde-musicd.service` (starts in every desktop session; self-gates on Workstation rank via ExecCondition + only where a user session exists, so headless Server/Lighthouse skip it), and the unit pins `Environment=MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` so it always answers the shared bus the GUIs use. Committed; **live-applied on .13** (user service enabled + active, boot-durable). Ships for fresh installs in the next roll. *(was: library only worked after a manual `mde-musicd serve`.)* Original below:

**MUSIC-DAEMON-AUTOSTART (original):** `mde-musicd` never runs on a Workstation, so the music library always fails with "daemon not responding".** Live on .13: the mde-music GUI publishes `action/music/*` to the bus; the responder is `mde-musicd serve`, which (a) ships **disabled** (the RPM `%post` enables mackesd/nebula/etc. but NOT mde-musicd) and (b) the shipped unit is a **system** service (`WantedBy=default.target`, runs as root with `HOME=/root`) while the Airsonic creds + the desktop bus live under the **uid-1000 desktop user** (`~/.local/share/mde/airsonic-creds.json`, `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus`) — so even if started it reads the wrong creds/bus. **Live-patched on .13** by launching `mde-musicd serve` as the desktop user with `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus`. **Durable fix:** make mde-musicd a **user service** (`systemctl --user`, role-gated to Workstation) that inherits the desktop user's HOME + bus, OR have the mde-music GUI spawn/supervise it; enable it in the `%post`/first-run for Workstation rank. **Acceptance:** on a fresh Workstation install, opening mde-music loads the library with no manual daemon start; the daemon reads the user's creds + the shared bus. *(the root-vs-user split mirrors the bus-root + QNM-Shared split-brain fixes.)* _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by the [✓] sibling above (~885); 0e1131e %post enables mde-musicd, live on .13)_

**VOIP-GW-1: Workbench area to set a mesh-wide SIP outbound gateway — SHIPPED.** New Mesh → "SIP Gateway" panel (host/port/user/password/display + Apply/Test/Clear) → `action/voip/set-gateway` → mackesd writes `<workgroup_root>/voip/gateway.toml` (QNM-Shared, 0600, account.toml shape) → every node's `mde-voice-hud` reads the mesh gateway first (`SipAccount::load`) and registers to it; bare numbers route out via it, peer calls stay P2P. Tests: 8 ipc/voip + 23 voice sip. *(committed `293f565`; age-encryption of the secret is a noted hardening follow-on. Ships in v10.0.12.)* Original ask below:

**VOIP-GW-1 (original story):** Operator (new feature): "Add an Area in the workbench to set a SIP outbound gateway for all mesh clients requiring an outbound gateway." A Provisioning/Voice panel in mde-workbench that configures ONE outbound SIP/PSTN gateway (registrar host, port, username, password/secret, realm, optional transport) and distributes it to every mesh client that needs an outbound trunk — i.e. writes the `account.toml` the `mde-voice-hud` agent already consumes (VOIP-P2P-4: present account.toml → registrar/trunk mode; absent → P2P-only). **Design:** store the gateway config as a leader-managed mesh secret on QNM-Shared (like the planned XCP creds, [[XCP-7]]) — `<QNM-Shared>/secrets/voip/gateway.age` (age-encrypted, never in `ps`/logs) — and each node's voice agent reads it to populate its `account.toml`; the panel has Apply + Test (REGISTER probe) like the Music panel. Intra-mesh peer calls stay P2P; bare numbers/URIs route out via this gateway. **Acceptance:** set the gateway once in the Workbench → every Workstation's voice agent registers to it and a bare-number call routes out via the gateway, while peer-name calls stay P2P; the secret is encrypted on QNM-Shared, absent from process listings. *(builds on the existing additive Asterisk trunk — VOIP-P2P-1/2/4; this is the missing config surface + mesh distribution.)* _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by the [✓] sibling above (~887); 293f565 SIP Gateway panel + action/voip/set-gateway, v10.0.12)_

**SELINUX-1: the platform daemon stack floods AVC denials under Enforcing → audit-log flood + repeating "SELinux security alert" toasts.** Live on .13: **32,521** `nnp_transition` denials in a single boot — `tokio-rt-worker` (mackesd, `unconfined_service_t`) transitioning to `container_runtime_t` (its `compute_*` workers shelling podman, whose runtime sets `no_new_privs`), plus libvirt `virtqemud_t` scanning mackesd's `/proc` and a stock `systemd_logind_t cap_userns:sys_ptrace` check. The denials never went to the journal (auditd captures them to `audit.log`), and `setroubleshootd`'s SEApplet re-surfaced the stored history as toasts across every reboot. **Fix (two layers):** (a) ship a local SELinux policy as **CIL modules** (`install-helpers/selinux/magicmesh-{base,podman,libvirt}.cil`) installed toolchain-free via `semodule -i` by `setup-selinux-policy.sh` from the RPM `%post` (all roles) — split by precondition so a missing optional type (no podman / no libvirt / older policy without `cap_userns`) skips only that module; the node STAYS Enforcing (never set permissive/disabled). (b) the toast surface no longer pops SELinux denials below Critical (`mde-notify-toast::should_toast` → `is_selinux_denial`): they record in the Security group of the Alert Center but don't toast (AVCs repeat across restarts + are not latency-critical), same policy as `fdo/*` app notifications. Tests: `selinux_denials_record_but_never_toast_below_critical`. **LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-06-17):** modules loaded + `getenforce`=Enforcing on all 4 nodes (.13, fedora, LH-01 all three; LH-02 podman+libvirt, base skipped — older policy, 0 denials there anyway); after `systemctl restart mackesd` on .13, **0** fresh AVC/`container_runtime_t` denials; setroubleshoot DB cleared + selinux_monitor checkpoint reset so the historical flood doesn't re-emit. **Acceptance:** a fresh-from-RPM node runs Enforcing with no mackesd-stack AVC denials; no repeating SELinux toast. *(durable rollout = the RPM `%post`; live-patched on all 4 now.)*

**LIGHTHOUSE-DNF-REPO (nit, found during the v10.0.12 roll) — FIXED LIVE.** Both lighthouses carried `/etc/yum.repos.d/mackes-mirror-magic-mesh.repo` → `baseurl=file:///root/QNM-Shared/mirrors/magic-mesh` (the MIRRORS/`mirror_syncd` local-mirror path, never populated on the droplets) → `dnf` `Curl error (37)` every transaction. Removed on both; dnf cache clean. *(If the local-mirror feature is wanted on the lighthouses, `mirror_syncd` must populate that path before the repo is re-added — otherwise the gh-pages channel is the package source. Filed as a note, not re-adding.)*
**SHELL-RPC-1** (`0db7753`) — healthz mount-enrichment bounded (timeout) so a wedged FUSE read can't hang the shell responder. *(live root cause was the lost leader-lock chunk; remediated.)*

**BULLETPROOF-1** (`46f5c11`) — bus retention GC now runs in mackesd + tmpfs-safe fs-relative cap + hard-cap eviction (prevents the /run-fill that bricked both lighthouses). *(hot-applied: lighthouses freed; fedora on new binary.)*

**NOTIFY-UI-4: the Action Center "wouldn't open" — its startup read of the QNM-Shared (FUSE) dir hung the iced update loop on a wedged mount.** Operator (.13, 2026-06-17): the notification area does not open; "continues when I attempt to open it." **Diagnosed live:** the running `mde-notify-center` (pid 13479) sat in the kernel `request_wait_answer` (a **FUSE** wait) with the loaded RSS but no mapped surface — `boot_task()` batches the layer-surface creation with the first `Message::Refresh`, whose handler called `AlertTail::poll_shared()` **inline on the update loop**; that reads `/mnt/mesh-storage/.mesh-alerts/` over LizardFS, and a wedged mount / lost-chunk-backed file blocks uninterruptibly → the loop never pumps the compositor roundtrip → the surface never maps. The applet bell (`pgrep`→`pkill`-or-spawn toggle) then just kills/respawns an instance that re-hangs at the same point, so it reads as "won't open." Same class as SHELL-RPC-1 (wedged FUSE read on the lost leader-lock chunk). **Fix:** split the read into a pure, state-free `mde_notify::read_shared_alert_items()` (safe on a helper thread) + `AlertTail::dedup_fresh()` (the `&mut self` dedup, on the caller). The center now runs the shared read on a helper thread and picks the result up **non-blockingly** via a `mpsc::Receiver` stored on `Center` — at most one read is ever in flight, the UI loop never blocks, the layer surface maps immediately regardless of mount health, and a recovered mount is picked up on a later poll. Tests: `read_shared_alert_items_is_pure_and_dedup_is_separate` (+ the existing `shared_alert_roundtrip_and_dedup` still green through the refactor). **Acceptance:** the Action Center opens on the bell within a poll cycle even when QNM-Shared is wedged; no `request_wait_answer`-hung center. *(deploy = build + roll the GUI binary to the Workstation nodes.)*

**NOTIFY-UI-3 / ICON-MESH: use the Networks icon for ALL Mesh File Services representations.** The desktop XDG folders (Documents/Downloads/… — bind-mounts of QNM-Shared) render with a local mounted-volume icon; they're mesh file services and should show a network icon. **Scope:** desktop folder icons (gio `metadata::custom-icon-name`/`folder-remote` set by the meshfs bind worker for the desktop user), mde-files mesh-storage representation, and the Workbench Mesh Storage panel. **Acceptance:** every mesh-file-service surface shows the network icon, not a local-volume icon. *(needs live desktop-icon iteration — cosmic-files icon-source behavior to confirm; not yet built.)*

**XPA-3 (golden identity reset: cloud-init clean + strip machine-id/host keys; pending golden run + clone-verify): cloud-init does NOT re-run on a cloned *booted* image → identity not reset.** All 4 clones kept the base's hostname (`f44-test`), machine-id, and SSH host keys despite a fresh NoCloud seed (new instance-id). A booted/customized farm template clone MUST be generalized before cloning (`cloud-init clean --logs --seed`, truncate `/etc/machine-id`, remove `/etc/ssh/ssh_host_*`) so each clone regenerates identity on first boot — OR the provisioner does an explicit post-clone identity reset (hostname + `systemd-machine-id-setup` + `ssh-keygen -A`). Build this into XCP-2 (golden) + XCP-3 (spawn).

**XPA-4 (in the clone recipe + provisioner TODO): clone copies the VIF MAC → collisions.** The provisioner must reset each clone's VIF (new MAC) — fold into XCP-3. _(reconciled 2026-06-30: 795f6a9 reset_vif_macs/mac_for_clone (locally-administered, collision-free) after clone)_

**XPA-5: enroll-token endpoint is the OVERLAY IP — VERIFIED + SUBSUMED BY [[XPA-7]] (2026-06-22).** `mackesd enroll-token` mints `mesh:…@10.42.0.2:4242#…` (the Nebula **overlay** IP), which a not-yet-enrolled LAN/internet node can't reach. The join token for an external node must carry a **publicly/LAN-reachable** enroll endpoint (the relay's public IP / the v3 `/enroll` HTTPS on :443). Confirm + fix the token-minting endpoint selection. _(reconciled 2026-06-30: subsumed by XPA-7 (795f6a9): provisioner mints v3 add-peer token pinning public /enroll + cert fp)_

**XPA-6: the monolithic RPM bundles all GUI binaries (~100 MB) — a headless Server install carries surfaces it never runs.** The full `cargo build --workspace --release` (libcosmic GUIs: workbench/music/voice-hud/applet) gates every install + a 100 MB+ transfer per node. Consider a `magic-mesh-server` subpackage (daemon + nebula + lizardfs-client, no GUI bins) for headless roles, or split GUI assets behind the role so a Server pulls only what it runs.

**XPA-7: use `mackesd add-peer` (v3) + `mackesd join`; NOT `enroll`/`enroll-token` (ENT-1).** `enroll` publishes a CSR to QNM-Shared + waits for a sign (needs the mount pre-existing — the MESH-1 chicken-and-egg) and `enroll-token` advertises the **overlay** IP (unreachable by a fresh node). `add-peer` mints a v3 token pinning the public `/enroll` endpoint (`:4243`) + cert fp — the only flow that works for an external node. Also: the `:443` covert path presents a DIFFERENT cert than `:4243` → `--enroll-port 443` fails `enroll-fingerprint-mismatch`; don't advertise :443 for enroll. **Provisioner (XCP-3) must use add-peer + join.**

**XPA-8 (CRITICAL — FIXED + VERIFIED on a clean VM-5): mackesd is hostage to `qnm-shared.service`.** `mackesd.service After=qnm-shared.service` + the qnm-shared mount-retry loop stuck "activating" → mackesd's start job **queues forever** (never runs, no journal, looks "inactive"). On a fresh node where the mount can't complete, the platform never starts. Make mackesd start **independently** of the mount (it already self-heals via `meshfs_worker`/BOOT-REC) — drop the hard `After=` to a `Wants=`/timeout, or give qnm-shared a bounded fail so it doesn't pin the queue.

**XPA-9 (CRITICAL — FIXED + VERIFIED: dnf auto-pulled fuse-libs on VM-5): the bundled fc43 lizardfs-client needs FUSE 2 (`libfuse.so.2`), absent on fresh F44.** `mfsmount: error while loading shared libraries: libfuse.so.2` → the QNM-Shared mount can NEVER succeed on a fresh Workstation/Server. The platform RPM must `Requires: fuse-libs` (+ `fuse` for the FUSE2 mount helper) — currently the lizardfs RPMs are bundled without their libfuse2 dep.

**XPA-10 (FIXED via `nonempty`): mackesd poisons the unmounted mountpoint → "mountpoint not empty" blocks the mount.** Because mackesd ran before the mount (XPA-8/9), it wrote `peer:*/`, `peers/`, `ssh-keys/` into `/mnt/mesh-storage` → mfsmount then refuses (non-empty). The BOOT-REC-1 `shared_root_writable` guard didn't prevent it on a node where the mount NEVER succeeded once. Harden: refuse writes to the canonical mountpoint until it is a verified FUSE mount; make qnm-shared's stray-clear reliable (it didn't keep up).
**XPA-11 (FIXED): `mackesd join` enroll transport intermittently times out on the first attempt (retry succeeds).** Add an internal retry/backoff to the enroll transport.

**XPA-12 (MET + VERIFIED on VM-5: `dnf install rpm` + `mackesd join --role server` → full member, QNM-Shared provisioned, nebula+mackesd+mesh-health up, in the directory, ZERO manual steps): there is NO single "install a Server + join" command.** Bringing one node to full membership took ~9 manual steps (dnf install, dnf fuse-libs, role-pin, mint token, join, stop services, clear mount stray, mfsmount, start mackesd). `mackesd join --role server` should **fully provision**: pull deps (fuse-libs), pin role, enroll, mount QNM-Shared, and start the daemon — one command → a member. This is the headline onboarding gap.

**XPA-13 (local-KVM path): a hand-built flow-style cloud-init seed breaks on the unquoted SSH key (spaces) → cloud-init silently drops user-data (no user/key/password).** Found provisioning MDE-KVM-1 on local KVM. Use block-style YAML with the key **quoted**, or `virt-install --cloud-init user-data=<file>` (builds the NoCloud datasource correctly). The XCP seeds used block-style + worked; the KVM one regressed via compact flow-style. Bake the block-style template into the provisioner (XCP-3) for both back-ends.
**BOOT-STATUS-1: mackesd `boot_readiness` worker — fabric steps + bus snapshot**

a `boot_readiness` worker probes, in order: Nebula up/cert, overlay-IP assigned, mackesd serving, mde-bus broker bound, QNM-Shared mounted+writable, peer directory replicated; emits an ordered `{id,label,group,status,detail,blocked_by,since_ms}` list.
publishes to `state/boot-readiness` on the bus each tick (fast while any step `pending`, backing off once steady).
**BOOT-STATUS-2: app-daemon probes + continuous liveness**

app-daemon `services` appended to the snapshot: musicd (mde-musicd unit), netdata (unit + :19999 reachability), KDE Connect (in-process listener, :1716 connect). (mde-voice-hud is a desktop GUI, not a boot daemon, so it is intentionally not a boot-readiness service — documented in `gather_services`.)

per-peer ping RTT (`pings`) to every directory peer incl. the lighthouse(s), gathered each 2 s tick with a bounded parallel fan-out (`ping -c1 -W1`, `parse_ping_rtt`, cap `MAX_PING_TARGETS`). The HOME panel consumes both (`BootService`/`BootPing`) so the data is reachable, not a dead payload.
**BOOT-STATUS-3: per-peer readiness roll-up**

each peer's reachability + RTT is aggregated into the snapshot `pings` and rendered as roll-up rows in the HOME boot section (✓/✕ + RTT, lighthouse tagged). (Health/degraded verdict refinement can layer onto these rows later.)
**BOOT-STATUS-4: Workbench HOME panel — dependency chain (reads state/boot-readiness)**

reconciled_disposition: Open candidate merged into reconciled active worklist or archived by report
title: subscribes to `state/boot-readiness`, repaints sub-second as steps transition pending→ok.
renders the boot-sequence dependency chain with always-shown sub-steps + per-peer roll-up rows.
collapses to a glanceable green "mesh ready" chip once all-green; re-expands on regression.
**BOOT-STATUS-5: auto-popup at desktop session start**

`packaging/autostart/org.magicmesh.BootStatus.desktop` (shipped via the existing autostart glob → `/etc/xdg/autostart/`; headless roles have no desktop session so it's naturally Workstation-only) runs `mde-workbench --boot-popup`, which opens at HOME (the default landing — the boot dependency chain renders there per BOOT-STATUS-4). It "minimizes to the chip on all-green" two ways: the `--boot-popup` guard (`boot_popup_should_suppress`) exits with NO window when the snapshot is already `ready`, and the HOME boot section itself collapses to the green "✓ Mesh ready" chip when all-green.
**BOOT-STATUS-6: inline remediation actions**

a down app-daemon row renders a **Restart** button wired to the real service-control path: `mde-musicd` (a per-user unit) restarts via `systemctl --user restart` with no privilege prompt — matching the acceptance example — while netdata + the in-process KDE Connect listener (hosted by `mackesd`) restart via `pkexec systemctl restart` (the same path the Mesh Services panel uses, `run_pkexec_systemctl`). The restart re-probes so the row flips to ✓; a one-line result (`restart ok` / `restart FAILED — see journalctl`) shows inline. **Retry** = the panel's existing Refresh (re-probe); **View-journal** = the System → Mesh Services panel (the existing `journalctl_tail` surface). Mapping is `service_remediation(id)`; pure helper + tests (`service_remediation_maps_known_daemons`). *(Fabric chain-step remediation — restarting nebula/mackesd/remount QNM mid-boot — is intentionally not auto-buttoned: those self-heal via mackesd's supervisors and a GUI-triggered restart of the transport mid-bringup is riskier than the app-daemon case the story targets.)*
**BOOT-PEERS-1: peer list "settling" state after a cold reboot**

while the fabric chain is incomplete the Peers panel shows a "Peers settling…" state (not the "empty mesh" guidance) — gated on `BootReadiness::fabric_converging()` (any non-`directory` boot step not yet ok), captured at load only when the roster is empty.

resolves to the live roster once peers appear; a lone healthy node (fabric up, no peers) still shows the genuine empty state (`fabric_converging` is false then). Builds on the BOOT-STATUS-2/3/4 snapshot; `BootStep` gained a stable `id` for robust keying.
**BUS-INODE-ORPHAN-1: the BOOT-REC-3 recreate must not orphan open consumers**

a recreate by one process does not wedge other live consumers — `Persist::reopen_if_index_changed` follows the swap; unit test `reopen_if_index_changed_follows_a_recreate` forces a recreate and confirms the second handle reopens and sees a write made post-recreate.

root fix first: `Persist::open` now prefers an **in-place perm fix** (chmod the shared-spool db + sidecars writable, re-probe) over the destructive unlink — no inode swap on the common boot-race; and the recreate is **gated on ownership** (`owns_or_root`, a portable self-chmod probe) so a uid-1000 GUI can't unlink the root daemon's live index. The MUSIC-WEDGE-2 reopen pattern is lifted into the shared `mde-bus` crate (`Persist::{index_inode,reopen_if_index_changed}`) and musicd now drives it; mackesd + workbench can adopt the same call.
the recreate / in-place fix / refusal each log loudly with the acting `pid` (BUS-INODE-ORPHAN-1 tracing targets).

**AFM-RECONNECT-1: mde-files cold-boot race left the peer roster empty forever.** DONE 2026-06-18: operator "Artifact Manager does not show any peers." Live triage: `action/fleet-files/peers` returned all 8 peers correctly — the daemon was fine. Root cause was GUI-side: `RealBackend::new()` probed the mesh + Fleet.Files backends with an 800ms timeout ONCE at launch and **cached the peer roster permanently**; a GUI opened before mackesd's responders were ready (cold boot) fell back to the local backend (empty peers) with no reconnect. **BOOT-XPA8-1: mackesd no longer queues behind the QNM-Shared mount loop.** DONE 2026-06-18. Root cause (critical-chain): `qnm-shared.service` runs a 60×2s (~120s) mfsmount-retry loop, and .13's **stale pre-XPA-8 units** gated mackesd on it (`qnm-shared` `Before=mackesd` + the `20-qnm.conf` drop-in `After=qnm-shared`) — so the apps service + whole mesh waited ~2 min for the mount before starting. `setup-qnm-shared.sh` already writes the decoupled (Wants-only) XPA-8 form, but **an RPM upgrade never re-ran it**, so nodes enrolled pre-XPA-8 kept the blocking ordering (infra-precondition gap). Fixes: (a) **live-patched .13** (removed `Before=mackesd` + the `After=` drop-in line, daemon-reload) — next reboot brings the apps service up in ~13s not ~2min; (b) **durable RPM `post_install` migration** (idempotent `sed` to strip the stale ordering on every upgrade) so the v10.0.18 roll fixes the whole fleet. **Disconnected-laptop answer:** with the gate removed, a laptop off-mesh boots straight to a usable local desktop + the apps service in ~13s; QNM-Shared/mesh features stay degraded and mount opportunistically when it reconnects — instead of the old ~2-min "everything down" stall every boot.

**BOOT-XPA8-2: qnm-shared retry is gentler on a permanently-disconnected node.** DONE 2026-06-19: mount-retry loop trimmed (~120s→~60s boot-race window) + `RestartSec=5`→`30` so an off-mesh node backs off 30s between failed cycles instead of spinning every 5s; mackesd isn't gated on it (XPA-8) + self-heals the mount, so connected boots are unaffected. RPM post_install migration bumps RestartSec on existing units too. The mount loop still burns ~120s then `Restart=on-failure` every 5s re-runs the full loop forever on an off-mesh laptop (wasteful, though no longer blocking). Make it back off (longer RestartSec, or hand the retry to the mesh-health watchdog / meshfs_worker) when the master is unreachable. **Acceptance:** a disconnected node doesn't spin a 2-min mount loop every few seconds; it retries on a slow cadence and mounts promptly once reconnected.

**BOOT-XPA8-3: the boot mount-retry loop displaced its own mfsmount → QNM-Shared never mounted after a reboot.** DONE 2026-06-19 (live on .13 + source). Operator: ".13 rebooted, mesh not reconnected after several minutes." Diagnosed: mackesd + nebula were up (XPA-8 worked) + the master (10.42.0.1) was reachable, but `qnm-shared` was stuck "activating" — the journal showed a new `mfsmount` every 2s, each "Received IO limits config" then "master: connection lost" (concurrent mount attempts colliding / a stale session displacing each new one). A single manual `mfsmount` succeeded immediately. Fix: the loop now `fusermount -u` + `pkill -f mfsmount <path>` (clear any stale/half-mount) BEFORE each attempt, and `sleep 3; mountpoint -q` AFTER to verify the mount actually held before exiting — so it never races itself. Live-patched .13's unit (+ RestartSec=30) and verified the mount + 10 peers + the directory RPC recovered; source `setup-qnm-shared.sh` updated (flows into v10.0.18).

**MOTION-CORE-3: global motion config + kill switch (P1).** **As** an operator, **I want** to disable/scale motion globally, **so that** I control the shell's feel. **Acceptance:** `[motion]` in preferences.toml (+ env override) toggles motion (off ⇒ terminal frames, zero ticks) and a speed scale measurably changes durations. *(deps: 1,2)*

**MOTION-INFRA-1: central Animator (one clock, registry, is_idle) (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** a shared animation driver, **so that** N animations run off ONE subscription that stops at rest. **Acceptance:** concurrent tweens advance off a single subscription; when all settle the subscription yields nothing (no `time::every` at idle). *(deps: Epic 1)*

**MOTION-INFRA-2: reusable enter/exit/crossfade/hover helpers (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** `fade_in`/`slide_in`/`crossfade`/`lift_on_hover`, **so that** surfaces animate uniformly via transform+opacity only. **Acceptance:** a panel mount fades+slides per token, reduce-motion collapses to instant/crossfade, no layout reflow during the transition. *(deps: INFRA-1)*

**MOTION-INFRA-3: frame-pacing + redundant-redraw avoidance + debug timing (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** ticks only while animating + visible, **so that** idle/offscreen burns nothing. **Acceptance:** at rest zero animation ticks (measured); hidden popup animates nothing; a debug flag reports per-frame ms. *(deps: INFRA-1)* *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*

**MOTION-FEEDBACK-1: buttons/tabs/nav/toolbar hover-focus-press (P0).** **As** a user, **I want** uniform hover-lift/press-depress/animated focus ring, **so that** controls feel alive + responsive. **Acceptance:** every interactive control shares the motion; reduce-motion keeps the state change without movement; press fires on down (no input delay). *(deps: Epic 2)*

**MOTION-FEEDBACK-2: cards/lists/tables selection + row state + staggered reveal (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** animated selection + capped stagger, **so that** lists feel fluid not jarring. **Acceptance:** selection animates the accent; a loaded list staggers in (≤8 cap); reduce-motion ⇒ instant. *(deps: FEEDBACK-1)*

**MOTION-FEEDBACK-3: menus/modals/drawers/notifications enter-exit (folds in APPS-FX-1 + NOTIFY-FX-1) (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** consistent popup/menu/Hub fade-scale, **so that** the whole shell shares one vocabulary. **Acceptance:** launcher, power menu, Hub, dialogs use the SAME enter/exit; reduce-motion ⇒ crossfade only; APPS-FX-1/NOTIFY-FX-1 are satisfied here (not duplicated). *(deps: FEEDBACK-1)*

**MOTION-NET-1: canonical async LoadState model (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** one `LoadState{Idle,Loading,Refreshing{stale},Degraded,Offline,Failed,Loaded}`, **so that** every async state is distinct + consistent. **Acceptance:** all 7 states render distinctly AND are legible without motion (text/icon) for a11y. *(deps: Epic 1)*

**MOTION-NET-2: skeleton + shimmer placeholders (folds in MUSIC-RESPONSIVE-6) (P0).** **As** a user, **I want** layout-matching skeletons while loading, **so that** slow loads never show blank. **Acceptance:** a slow first load shows skeletons matching the eventual layout; shimmer stops on data; reduce-motion ⇒ static grey. *(deps: NET-1, Epic 2)*

**MOTION-NET-3: stale-while-refreshing + smooth data replacement (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** stale data kept (dimmed) with a refresh indicator then crossfaded, **so that** a refresh never blanks the view. **Acceptance:** refresh never blanks the panel; old→new crossfades; a background-refresh indicator shows. *(deps: NET-1,2)*

**MOTION-NET-4: optimistic updates + graceful retry (folds in MUSIC-RESPONSIVE-8) (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** actions to apply instantly + reconcile, **so that** the UI feels fast. **Acceptance:** a transport toggle flips instantly + reconciles; a failed action reverts + offers retry without losing context. *(deps: NET-1)*

**MOTION-NET-5: background-poll + connection-degraded indicators (P2).** **As** an operator, **I want** background work + degraded connectivity visible, **so that** I trust the shell's state. **Acceptance:** background refresh shows a non-blocking indicator; bus/mesh down shows a degraded state that auto-recovers. *(deps: NET-1)*

**MOTION-TRANS-1: route/panel switch transitions (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** panel/page switches to crossfade, **so that** large view changes feel intentional. **Acceptance:** panel switches crossfade; reduce-motion ⇒ instant; no input delay. *(deps: Epic 2)*

**MOTION-TRANS-2: panel expand/collapse + drawer + modal open/close (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** eased expanders/drawers/modals, **so that** they don't pop. **Acceptance:** each uses its token preset consistently; reduce-motion safe. *(deps: FEEDBACK-3)*

**MOTION-TRANS-3: list insert/remove + table refresh transitions (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** rows to reveal/collapse + tables to refresh smoothly, **so that** updates aren't abrupt. **Acceptance:** inserted rows reveal, removed rows collapse, a table refresh doesn't jump scroll. *(deps: FEEDBACK-2, NET-3)*

**MOTION-TRANS-4: split-pane + window-resize polish, Wayland (P3).** **As** a user, **I want** smooth split-drag + resize, **so that** the shell feels native. **Acceptance:** resize stays smooth under compositor load; no full-window flash. *(deps: Epic 6)* _(BLOCKED: no resize-motion code yet, and the acceptance is real-Cosmic-session compositor behaviour that can't be verified in a headless build — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_

**MOTION-PERF-1: idle/offscreen tick suppression (P0, gates the rest).** **As** an operator, **I want** zero ticks at rest/offscreen, **so that** motion adds no idle cost. **Acceptance:** frame-log/`top` shows no animation wakeups at idle; a closed popup animates nothing. *(deps: INFRA-3)*

**MOTION-PERF-2: transform/opacity-only, no layout thrash (P1).** **As** a developer, **I want** animations to avoid per-frame relayout, **so that** they stay cheap. **Acceptance:** profiling shows no per-frame relayout during transitions. *(deps: Epic 2)*

**MOTION-PERF-3: frame-time + redraw instrumentation (P2).** **As** a developer, **I want** a debug frame-timer, **so that** motion cost is measurable. **Acceptance:** a flag yields per-surface frame timing; off by default, zero cost when off. *(deps: INFRA-3)*

**MOTION-PERF-4: HiDPI/fractional-scaling correctness + stress validation (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** motion correct at fractional scale + under load, **so that** it never jitters/clips. **Acceptance:** no clipping/blur/jitter at 1.0/1.25/1.5/2.0; smooth under GPU/CPU/network stress (on a real Cosmic session). *(deps: Epic 2)* _(BLOCKED: no code artifact, and the acceptance is an explicit hardware/live-Cosmic-session compositor verification that can't be asserted in a headless build — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_

**MOTION-A11Y-1: wire reduce-motion through every consumer (P0).** **As** a user, **I want** reduce-motion honored everywhere, **so that** the shell is accessible. **Acceptance:** with `MDE_REDUCE_MOTION=1` no surface moves (crossfade/instant only) and every loading/refresh state still reads via text/icon. *(deps: Epics 1,2)*

**MOTION-A11Y-2: disable non-essential motion + respect system prefs (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** to drop decorative motion but keep state cues, **so that** I tune comfort. **Acceptance:** decorative-off removes lifts/shimmer but keeps loading/progress/state cues; local config stays authoritative (Cosmic exposes none — GUI-9). *(deps: A11Y-1)*

**MOTION-A11Y-3: no flashing / bounded pulse + keyboard/SR semantics (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** no flashing + intact a11y semantics during motion, **so that** it's safe. **Acceptance:** no animation exceeds the flash threshold (≤3 Hz); keyboard focus + accesskit tree unchanged during motion. *(deps: A11Y-1)*

**MOTION-AUDIT-1: inventory static/no-feedback components (P1).** **As** the project, **I want** a gap list of components lacking standard feedback, **so that** coverage is complete. **Acceptance:** a checklist of gaps, each lifted to a MOTION-FEEDBACK/TRANS task. *(deps: Epics 3,5)*

**MOTION-AUDIT-2: replace duplicate/one-off animation logic with shared primitives (P1).** **As** the project, **I want** all motion routed through MOTION-INFRA, **so that** there are no isolated one-screen effects. **Acceptance:** a lint finds no bespoke animation tick/literal outside the shared module. *(deps: Epic 2)*

**MOTION-AUDIT-3: document the motion language + contributor examples (P2).** **As** a contributor, **I want** a motion guide with working snippets, **so that** future work stays consistent. **Acceptance:** the guide covers every pattern with a compiling snippet; referenced from AI_GOVERNANCE §4. *(deps: Epics 1–7)*
