**LH-JOIN-QNM-1: a fresh remote lighthouse join leaves `/mnt/mesh-storage` a wedged FUSE mount (never mounts).** _(BLOCKED: both code fixes landed (wedge-proof mount loop + source-side share guard); the two remaining acceptance bullets both require the fix shipped in an RPM + re-verified on a real DO lighthouse / VM bed — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_
the `qnm-shared` mount loop is wedge-proof (timeout-guarded checks + `-uz`/`umount -l` lazy clear) with a bounded `TimeoutStartSec` so it can't be SIGKILLed or hang `activating` forever

a fresh `mackesd join --role lighthouse` (fixed script deployed) ends with `/mnt/mesh-storage` mounted + the full shared roster readable, **no reboot, no manual intervention** — *needs the fix shipped in an RPM (or the patched script staged on the droplet pre-join), then a re-provision*

**the SOURCE-SIDE guard is the real fix (PRIORITY).** **GUARD LANDED (2026-06-21, code + unit test):** `ssh_pubkey_gossip.rs` — the SVC-2 worker published `<root>/ssh-keys/<host>.pub` into the workgroup root with **no** `shared_root_writable` check (meshfs_worker had one; this did not), so on the canonical mountpoint-not-mounted it `create_dir_all`'d the bare local dir and poisoned it. Added a `share_writable()` seam (`crate::shared_root_writable(&self.workgroup_root)`) and an early-return guard at the publish site in `gossip_one` so the share is a quiet no-op until genuinely mounted (also preserves the existing `authorized_keys` block instead of pruning against an empty share). Regression test `share_writable_gates_on_real_mount_for_canonical_root` (8/8 gossip tests green; `cargo check` + clippy + mesh-boundary lint clean, gold-linker local build). **Still needs (live, can't run from the airgapped dev box):** verify in the SUBSTRATE-14 VM bed that a fresh `mackesd join --role lighthouse` now mounts on the first try, then a real DO re-provision. *(Background finding kept below.)* A live re-provision (2026-06-20, DO Lighthouse-03/04, fixed script staged) proved the timeout+wedge fixes are **necessary but NOT sufficient**: with `TimeoutStartSec=600` the unit no longer SIGKILLs and stays `activating` (looping), but the mount still never establishes (12+ retry iterations, `mounted=NO`). The mount loop can't win against a **relentless stray-write race** — mackesd/the join writes `peer:/peers/ssh-keys/validation` into the unmounted `/mnt/mesh-storage` continuously, and the loop's clear→mount + a concurrent manual mount keep re-wedging the FUSE mount (every access D-state-hangs). The exact mfsmount error was never capturable live (the node wedges under any concurrent mount). **Fix: find + guard the unguarded join/setup writer** (meshfs_worker IS `shared_root_writable`-guarded; the BIRTHRIGHT-1 join path that seeds `peer:/peers/ssh-keys/validation` is NOT) so the path stays empty until mounted — then the loop mounts cleanly on the first try. **Verify in the controlled VM bed** (SUBSTRATE-14-style), NOT on a live DO node that wedges under concurrent attempts. *(Also re-confirm whether lizardfs mfsmount's `-o nonempty` actually works on the F43 fuse 2.9.9 build, or drop it in favor of guaranteed-empty-then-mount.)*

**FOUND-NEBULA-1: stock `/etc/nebula/config.yml` broke a fresh v11 lighthouse founding.** DONE 2026-06-20. **Found live** standing up a clean v11 lighthouse on a fresh F43 DO droplet: `mackesd found` minted the CA + lighthouse cert and the nebula-supervisor materialized `/etc/nebula/config.yaml` (am_lighthouse:true, correct pki), but the **`nebula` Fedora package ships an example `/etc/nebula/config.yml`** (am_lighthouse:false, bogus `192.168.100.1` static_host_map, pki=host.crt). The nebula unit runs `-config /etc/nebula` (the whole DIRECTORY), so nebula MERGED the stale example with our config.yaml → the example won → overlay never formed (unit failed). Same bug would hit every fresh `mackesd join` (incl. eagle). **Fix:** `materialize_config` now removes a pre-existing stock `config.yml` after writing `config.yaml` (regression test `materialize_removes_stock_nebula_config_yml`). Verified live: removing it + restart brought overlay `10.42.0.1` up. Ships in 11.0.1.

**FOUND-NEBULA-4: a fresh `found` never materializes `/etc/nebula` without a working QNM-Shared mount → no overlay.** FIXED + verified live 2026-06-22 (surfaced by the BUILD-PLATFORM-5 L2 mini-mesh). `cmd_found` minted the CA + bundle then enabled `nebula.service`, but the `/etc/nebula` materialization (ca.crt/host.crt/host.key + rendered `config.yaml` + removal of the stock `config.yml`) is done by the **leader-gated** `nebula_supervisor` worker, which materializes only on leader-promotion. The legacy leader lock lives on `/mnt/mesh-storage/.mackesd-leader.lock`, so a founder that hasn't mounted QNM-Shared (the MESH-1 chicken-and-egg; the mount SUBSTRATE-V2 is removing) can never take leadership → the supervisor never runs → nebula starts against the stock `config.yml` (pki→non-existent `host.crt`) → crash-loops to start-limit `failed` → no overlay. (FOUND-NEBULA-1's stock-removal lives *inside* `materialize_config`, so it never fires when `materialize_config` itself never runs.) **Fix:** `found` now materializes inline — `read_bundle` + `materialize_config(…, ConfigRole::Host)` before starting nebula, mirroring the join path (`persist_bundle`); a node's own `/etc/nebula` is a local concern, correctly ungated by cluster leadership; the supervisor re-renders identically later (idempotent). **Verified live:** hot-swapped the fixed binary onto a clean founded node → `/etc/nebula` has ca.crt/host.crt/host.key/config.yaml (stock `config.yml` removed), `nebula.service` active, `nebula1` up at `inet 10.42.0.1/17`. Commit 0435bf1; aligned with SUBSTRATE-V2 (overlay no longer depends on the LizardFS mount). **CONFIRMED FROM THE RPM 2026-06-22:** the L2 mini-mesh, run against a clean farm-cut RPM containing the fix, now reports **"node A serve raised the overlay (10.42.x)" PASS** — a fresh `found` of the released RPM forms the founding lighthouse's overlay (was FAIL before the fix). The fix is validated end-to-end, not just via hot-swap.

**FOUND-NEBULA-5: a fresh `mackesd join` 401'd — bearer-ledger root mismatch between the sudo CLI and the systemd daemon. FIXED + verified live 2026-06-23.** Captured B's real `join` stderr on a 2-node bed: `HTTP 401: enrollment of peer:… refused: its bearer is not issued-and-unredeemed`. **Root cause:** `add-peer` (run as `sudo mackesd add-peer`) writes the issued-bearer ledger under `default_workgroup_root()`, but `sudo` strips `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT` so the CLI fell through to the `~/QNM-Shared` fallback → it wrote `/root/QNM-Shared/ca/issued-bearers/<hash>.json`. The serve process's `/enroll` listener (systemd unit sets `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT=/mnt/mesh-storage`) validated against `/mnt/mesh-storage/ca/issued-bearers/` (unmounted/empty) → `is_pending` false → 401. Same MESH-1 class as FOUND-NEBULA-4 (CLI vs daemon resolving the QNM-Shared root differently on a fresh node). **Fix:** `mackes_mesh_types::peers::default_workgroup_root()` env-less fallback → the canonical `/mnt/mesh-storage` (was `~/QNM-Shared`), so CLI + daemon agree byte-for-byte mounted-or-not. **Confirmed live:** mirroring A's ledger into the daemon's root made B join immediately (`joined l2test as peer:mcnf-test-1, overlay 10.42.0.2`); the code fix makes that automatic. **CONFIRMED FROM THE RPM 2026-06-23:** a fresh capstone RPM (both FOUND-NEBULA-4 + -5 + the full batch) drove the L2 mini-mesh from **2/7 → 5/7** — every ONBOARDING step now passes: found-A · add-peer · **A overlay** · **B joins** · **B overlay up**. The onboarding path is fixed end-to-end. *(Note: the legacy `qnm-shared.service` LizardFS mount still fails non-fatally on a fresh join — SUBSTRATE-V2 removes it; tracked there.)*

**FOUND-NEBULA-6: data-plane — reachability WORKS; the peer directory doesn't federate on a bare bed (diagnosed 2026-06-23).** Re-ran the 2-node onboard with NO teardown + captured nebula handshake logs: **A↔B reachability is NOT broken** — the Nebula ix_psk0 handshake completes both directions and `A ping 10.42.0.2 = 0% packet loss`. The L2 failure was a TEST artifact: the harness pinged once, immediately, against an IP it sourced from `mackesd peers` — but (i) the tunnel needs a few seconds to handshake after both serve, and (ii) `mackesd peers` lists only A on a bare bed, so the ping had no target. **Harness fixed** (`test-feature.sh`): reachability now retries `ping 10.42.0.2` (B's deterministic IP) for ~24s, independent of the directory → it now passes. **The one real remaining gap is the directory**: `mackesd peers` shows only A because the peer registry federates over the shared substrate (QNM-Shared/etcd), absent on a bare bed — squarely **SUBSTRATE-3** (peer directory on etcd). So the bare-bed L2 ceiling is **6/7**; the 7th (directory) closes when SUBSTRATE-V2 lands. **Acceptance:** 6/7 on a bare bed now; 7/7 after SUBSTRATE-3. **RESOLVED 2026-06-23 via the SUBSTRATE-3 bed rehearsal:** provisioned a 2-member etcd cluster over the overlay (`setup-etcd --init`/`--join` on 10.42.0.1/.2) + restarted mackesd → `mackesd peers` on A now federates **BOTH** nodes (mcnf-test-0 + mcnf-test-1) over etcd, i.e. the 7th L2 step passes once etcd is provisioned. Leader election simultaneously moved off the lockfile onto the etcd lease ("acquired/renewed mesh leadership lease (etcd)"). So the directory is NOT a product gap — it federates over the (already-built) etcd substrate; the only reason a bare L2 bed sees 6/7 is that etcd auto-provisioning on found/join is intentionally deferred (the operator-gated big-bang cutover, SUBSTRATE-14). *(marked `[!]` = the autonomous part is done + validated; closing to 7/7 unattended needs the operator's cutover go-ahead.)* *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*

**INCIDENT-WEDGE-1: fleet-wide LizardFS FUSE wedge + recovery runbook.** During the v11 rebuild, destroying the old DO lighthouses removed the LizardFS master, so EVERY old-mesh node's QNM-Shared mounts (`/mnt/mesh-storage`, `/root/QNM-Shared`, XDG bind-mounts) wedged at once — `mfs#<gone-master>:9421` mounts pinning each box at load 80+ with uninterruptible D-state procs that `kill -9` can't touch and that block sudo/rpm/systemd. **Key finding:** `fusermount -uz` does NOT release the blocked I/O; aborting the FUSE connection via `/sys/fs/fuse/connections/*/abort` does (drops load 82→10 in seconds). Packaged as `install-helpers/unwedge-lizardfs.sh` (abort → lazy-unmount → mask qnm-shared; stragglers need `reboot -f`). **This is the SUBSTRATE-V2 motivation in one incident:** with etcd+Syncthing there is no FUSE master to disappear. Reinforces OPROG-2 (etcd cutover) urgency: a node rejoining the new mesh must keep `qnm-shared` masked.

title: **BUG-VIDEO-1: Media/video player won't play a local video on Eagle.** **As** a user, **I want** to open a video file from my home dir and have it play, **so that** the Media surface is usable locally. **Root cause (live-verified 2026-07-03):** `.13` runs magic-mesh **11.4.5**, whose RPM ships **no `mde-media-egui` binary** (the MEDIA player epic is a 12.x feature; `command -v mde-media-egui` = absent). The local files are `~/Downloads/*.mkv` = **AV1 video (libdav1d) + Opus audio in Matroska**; `libmpv.so.2` + gstreamer + ffmpeg are all present on the box. **Acceptance:** deploy a **12.x** build to `.13` so the Media surface/player exists; the player opens a local `~/Downloads/*.mkv` and plays it (video + audio); confirm the player's decode path handles **AV1+Opus/Matroska** (libmpv-backed → uses system dav1d) — if the 12.x player can't decode AV1, that's a follow-on codec task. Verified live on Eagle: a local .mkv plays. *(Same old-build root cause as BUG-STORAGE-1 — folded into the `.13` 12.x redeploy; also recon `crates/desktop/mde-media-egui` decode backend to confirm AV1 before deploying.)* *(⚠️ RECON VERDICT 2026-07-03 — a straight 12.x deploy will NOT play it; the codec is a red herring (mpv+dav1d on `.13` decodes AV1+Opus/mkv fine). TWO build/wiring blockers: **(A) shipped player is `FakeMpv`** — the `mpv` feature (`mde-media-core/Cargo.toml` `mpv=["dep:libmpv2"]`, default off) is never enabled in any build path (`xcp-build.sh:448` builds `--features drm`; `build-rpm-fedora43.sh:100` default features), so the embedded player simulates playback (flips to Playing, 0:00 frozen, no A/V). FIX: add `media-mpv=["mde-media-egui/mpv"]` to `mde-shell-egui/Cargo.toml`, build `--features drm,media-mpv`, and provision **`mpv-libs-devel`** on the farm build VMs (links system libmpv at build — farm lacks it, honest-gated). **(B) video stage is a placeholder** — `mde-media-egui/src/app.rs:1324` `player_stage` paints a dark rect; no mpv-render-API→egui-texture upload and no DRM overlay-plane scanout (MEDIA-2 shipped only as a seam). FIX: implement the frame→display path. ⚠️ **DESIGN FORK (operator): render via mpv render-API → egui `TextureHandle` upload, vs a DRM overlay plane beneath the egui shell.** Local-file open + surface mount are FINE (MEDIA-18 done; Open-URL/Index-folder accept `~/Downloads/*.mkv`). Storage+brightness are unaffected by this — the `.13` 12.x deploy still fixes those two.)* **L0+L1 VALIDATION SLICE 2026-07-10 (`docs/gpu_encoder.md`):** the design fork is resolved as **texture upload, not the render API** — `mde-media-core`/`mde-media-egui` are workspace `unsafe_code = "forbid"`, and libmpv2 6.0's render API is OpenGL-only (verified against the upstream `kohsine/libmpv2-rs` source), so driving it would need an off-screen EGL/GL context built from `unsafe` FFI these crates cannot carry; the DRM-overlay path is explicitly phase 2 and stays out of scope. Instead `MediaEngine` grew `latest_frame(&mut self) -> Option<VideoFrame>` (`mde-media-core/src/engine.rs`): `MpvEngine::latest_frame` (`src/mpv.rs`) captures via mpv's `screenshot-to-file "video"` command (throttled ~150 ms) and decodes the PNG with the same `png = "0.18"` path `mde-shell-egui`'s `chooser::decode_png_rgba` already uses — no GPU/EGL context needed at all (confirmed against mpv's own `video/out/vo.c` + `player/client.c` sources: frame retention for screenshots is generic core-VO behaviour, not per-driver, so it works under `vo=null`; the synchronous `mpv_command` client API blocks until the spawned screenshot-writer thread finishes, so there is no read-after-write race). `MpvEngine::new` now also sets `vo=null` (the shell owns the one DRM/KMS master; mpv must never grab its own display, extending the existing "don't fight the seat for a resource" comment already covering terminal/input) and `audio-fallback-to-null=yes` (a missing/broken audio device degrades to silent video instead of aborting the file). `FakeMpv::latest_frame` stays an honest `None` unless a test scripts one via new `with_frame()`. **Release wiring:** `media-mpv = ["mde-media-egui/mpv"]` landed on `mde-shell-egui/Cargo.toml`; `xcp-build.sh`'s `rpm` recipe and `build-rpm-fedora43.sh` both now build the shell `--features drm,live-helper,live-vdi,media-mpv` (previously `drm`-only / default-features — the exact gap the recon named) and `dnf install mpv-libs-devel` (resolves via Fedora 42's `mpv-devel`/`mpv-libs` 0.40.0) alongside the other native build deps; `setup-build-vm-toolchain.sh` provisions it on the base toolchain too. **L0 production-feature-detection test** (the doc calls this out explicitly): `release_shell_configuration_enables_the_real_media_engine` (`mde-shell-egui/src/main.rs`) asserts `!cfg!(feature="drm") || cfg!(feature="media-mpv")` — always compiled, only a real constraint once `drm` is on. **L1 fixture-decode test**: `mde-media-core/tests/mpv_fixture_decode.rs` (`#![cfg(feature = "mpv")]`, mirrors the `media_smoke` real-clip honest-gate shape) drives a real `MpvEngine` (`TypeId`-checked, never `FakeMpv`) against a checked-in ~14 KB synthetic fixture (`tests/fixtures/tiny_clip.mkv` — VP8 video + Opus audio in Matroska, `ffmpeg` `testsrc2`/`sine` lavfi sources, chosen for decoder portability over codec coverage — AV1 stays the standing follow-on codec task the original recon already named); asserts `Playing`/`Ended` is reached, `current-ao` resolves (a real device or the honest `null` fallback), and `latest_frame()` returns a nonblank frame (pixel-chunk comparison, not raw-byte — a fixed bug the farm run itself caught) with a nonzero, stable FNV-1a checksum. **Frame sink**: `player_stage` (`mde-media-egui/src/app.rs`) now uploads `latest_frame()` output through a new `VideoTextureCache` (an `egui::TextureHandle`, mirroring `mde-shell-egui/src/vdi.rs`'s `VdiState.texture` allocate-once/`set`-after pattern) and paints it in place of the placeholder rect once loaded, with the Carbon OSD scrim/title unchanged on top; `media_panel`/`player_view`/`MediaApp`/the shell's `Shell` struct all thread the cache through. **Farm evidence (`.130` BigBoy, `libmpv` freshly provisioned live via the sanctioned `xcp-build.sh rpm`-style remote `sudo dnf install` pattern, then landed in the provisioning scripts above):** `cargo build -p mde-media-core -p mde-media-egui -p mde-shell-egui` (default features) clean; `cargo test` same scope → 231+85+959 passed/0 failed (incl. the new `VideoFrame`/`FakeMpv::latest_frame`/`render_with_video` unit tests and the detection test trivially passing with `drm` off); `cargo build -p mde-media-core --features mpv` clean (confirms the real link once `mpv-libs-devel` is present); `cargo test -p mde-media-core --features mpv` → `fixture_decodes_through_real_mpv_with_a_nonblank_frame` passed (0.29s) + 231 existing passed; `cargo build -p mde-media-egui --features mpv` clean; `cargo build -p mde-shell-egui --features drm,live-helper,live-vdi,media-mpv` clean (the exact RPM release combination); `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm,media-mpv` → `release_shell_configuration_enables_the_real_media_engine` + `shell_mounts_and_renders_the_media_surface` both passed (959 total); **negative proof** `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm` (no `media-mpv`) → the detection test FAILS with the intended diagnostic, proving the gate is real, not tautological; `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean on every touched file across all four feature combinations (a handful of pre-existing `mpv.rs` pedantic/nursery warnings — never clippy-checked before, since `mpv-libs-devel` had never been on the farm — fixed in passing: doc-markdown backticks, the track-count cast, `end_reason` as `const fn`, one documented `#[allow(clippy::match_same_arms)]` in `poll()`); `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` clean on every touched file. **Remaining:** **L2** — a full `xcp-build.sh rpm` cut proving the generated RPM actually ships `media-mpv` + `rpm -qlp` evidence (not attempted here; the doc's own heavier gate) — note the shared default farm slot showed unrelated concurrent drift in `Cargo.lock` from other work, so re-sync before that cut. **L3** — live-seat validation on Eagle/`.2`: open a real `~/Downloads/*.mkv`, confirm frames visibly change over the whole clip (not just one capture), audio through PipeWire, pause/seek/OSD controls, and specifically **AV1** decode (the L1 fixture used VP8 for portability, so AV1 coverage is still the standing follow-on codec task). The DRM overlay plane (MEDIA-2 phase 2 performance path) remains explicitly unattempted, per the doc's own recommended path. *(branch `worktree-agent-a101e274e40e25d46`, not pushed — operator review pending)*

title: **NAVBAR-U3: active sessions as temporary bar entries.** **As** a user, **I want** my open desktops in the bar, **so that** I can switch like a taskbar. **Acceptance:** each connected remote desktop shows a live glyph in the bar (a 'desktops' run), appearing on connect + removed on disconnect; click focuses that session; a session/count badge (reuse NAVBAR-5). **PARTIAL SLICE 2026-07-07:** the bottom rail first rendered the current Desktop request from the existing `VdiState::requested_summary()` as a named taskbar-style session entry (`SessionRailEntry`, `IconId::Sessions`, target label + protocol), immediately after Desktop and before the right tray; clicking it focuses `Surface::Desktop`. With no concrete target it preserves the old dim generic Sessions micro-icon. **BROKER-LOG SLICE 2026-07-07:** added `session_rail.rs`, a shell-local projection of the broker's public `action/vdi/session` JSON wire. The shell now folds Open/Active/Disconnect/Close actions from the Bus log, filters to this client peer, and feeds non-closed entries into the bottom rail before falling back to the pending `VdiState` request. This consumes the broker wire shape without depending on `mackesd` or creating a parallel session type; `SessionStore`/etcd remains the live multi-seat authority when it lands. **FOCUS SLICE 2026-07-07:** broker-backed rail entries now carry their `SessionId`; clicking one latches that id through `DockState::take_desktop_session_focus()`, routes to `Surface::Desktop`, and marks the local rail projection live for that session without publishing a fake broker lifecycle transition. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui session_rail::tests::broker_session_actions_fold_into_local_rail_entries -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui session_rail::tests::focused_session_entry_marks_the_local_rail_entry_live -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::a_requested_desktop_session_renders_as_a_named_bottom_rail_entry -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi::tests::requested_summary_names_the_pending_desktop_and_protocol -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` (38 passed). **Remaining:** true cross-seat/multi-session truth requires the broker/roaming `SessionStore` live etcd/Syncthing session directory; this slice reads the local Bus action log and does not claim the shared plane is live.

**NAVBAR-U4: one state, two faces.** DONE 2026-07-07. The shell owns one `ChooserState` and uses it for both faces: `chooser_panel(ui, &mut self.chooser)` is the expanded Desktop chooser, while `self.chooser.rail_sources()` and `self.chooser.connect_source_id()` feed and mutate the compact bottom-rail flyout. The compact face returns only a source id through `DockState`; the shell hands that id back to `ChooserState`, so protocol choice, credentials, favorites, recents, manual sources, pending prompts, and connect handoff all stay in the one chooser model. Added a regression proving a compact selection of an external endpoint without a sealed credential raises the pending credential prompt in the same `ChooserState` that the expanded `chooser_panel` renders. `session_rail.rs` remains separate on purpose: it mirrors the broker's public VDI session log for NAVBAR-U3 entries and does not duplicate chooser source state. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chooser::tests::compact_selection_and_expanded_panel_share_the_same_pending_picker -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chooser::tests::compact -- --nocapture` (3 passed). **Acceptance:** compact and expanded chooser faces read/mutate the same `ChooserState`; no second source store; tested pending-state continuity across compact→expanded.

title: **KDC-MESH-4: QR pair-to-mesh onboarding.** **As** an operator, **I want** one scan to enroll+pair, **so that** onboarding is trivial. **QR PAYLOAD/RENDER SLICE 2026-07-07:** the Phones hub Pair tab now renders a real in-shell QR matrix using a compact `mde-kdc-mesh-pair:<base64url-json>` payload with versioned `enroll` and `pair` fields. The `pair` field carries the existing overlay KDC target (`mde-kdc-pair:<device-id>@<overlay-ip>:1716`); the `enroll` field carries a pasted short-TTL token from `mackesd found` or `mackesd onboard invite-issue` when the operator provides one, an automatically daemon-minted `mde-invite:` token when available, and is honestly `null` for pair-only bring-up until a fresh token is published. The raw payload is visible/copyable for scanner bring-up, and the QR is painted directly from the encoder matrix through egui rectangles (no fake bitmap asset). Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui phones_hub::tests::pair_qr_payload_carries_enroll_and_pair_fields -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui phones_hub::tests::pair_qr_payload_honestly_allows_pending_enroll_token -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui phones_hub::tests::normalized_enroll_token_trims_manual_token_for_the_qr -- --nocapture`. **AUTO TOKEN SLICE 2026-07-10:** `kdc_host` now serves `action/connect/mesh-enroll-token`, mints a short-TTL onboarding invite through the existing onboard invite issuer, records it in the bearer ledger, publishes latest state to `state/connect/mesh-enroll-token`, and replies with the same token; the Phones hub automatically requests a token when the manual override is empty and no fresh daemon token is present or the token is close to expiry, then folds the reply into the Pair QR. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-kdchost` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `57/57`; `.50` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd mesh_enroll_token --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `2/2`; `.90` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui enroll_token -- --nocapture` passed `8/8`; `.170` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-fmt` farm-side touched-file `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check` passed. **PAIRING RECORD SLICE 2026-07-10:** `kdc_host` now has daemon-boundary coverage for `action/connect/pair-device`: the regression spins up a loopback TLS phone, drives the same sync Bus verb handler the shell uses, proves TOFU captures the presented fingerprint, writes the canonical pairing record, persists the sealed session, and survives reopening `PairingStore`. title: **WIN7-DESKTOP-1: refactor the left sidebar and top status bar into a Windows 7 Desktop Layout.** **As** the operator, **I want** the shell chrome reshaped around a Windows 7 desktop idiom, **so that** the desktop feels familiar while retaining Quasar's live operational surfaces. **Acceptance:** the current left sidebar and top status bar are replaced or upgraded into a Windows 7-style desktop layout; all existing color tiles remain and become live tiles on the Start Menu; the Advanced menu content fills the rest of the upgraded Start Menu; Carbon theme tokens remain authoritative; the work begins with a 150-question one-at-a-time multiple-choice survey to lock layout, density, live-tile behavior, menu grouping, status placement, accessibility, and migration constraints before implementation. **SURVEY LOCKED 2026-07-10:** worked a 23-question one-at-a-time survey (the operator opted for a targeted subset over the full 150 once every required axis had a real answer) — full locks + architecture + an 11-unit (WIN7-1..11) plus surface-complete-gate (WIN7-12) draining plan in `docs/design/win7-desktop-survey.md`. Headline locks: a true bottom taskbar (Start · sessions · tray · clock) with the Start Menu opening upward as a fixed-size two-pane panel (live tiles left, the existing Console panel's content migrated + redesigned right, Power section anchored bottom); real Win8-style rotating live tiles in function-based groups; denser-than-baseline chrome; full accesskit parity from the first slice; the NOTIF-6 critical edge-cue now also auto-closes the Start Menu when it fires. One unanticipated addition the survey surfaced: a CEF-rendered desktop/wallpaper layer (not native egui) — primary source is an operator-configured local URL for a separate project (`MACKESCODE-AI-PROXY`, installed independently of this repo), falling back to Bing's daily picture (on-by-default network call — a deliberate, called-out exception to this project's usual airgap-default convention for external-network features, kept individually toggleable). Two things intentionally left open per the design doc: the hotkey scheme itself (locked as "clean break," not yet surveyed in detail) and where Console currently persists its Custom-group entries (needs verifying before the multi-seat-sync unit can be scoped). **WIN7-1 DONE 2026-07-10:** relocated the bottom-taskbar chrome per lock #3 — but investigation found `dock.rs`'s own top-of-file doc comment (and 3 other in-file comments) were stale: the Start cell (`start_cell`), the NAVBAR-U1/U2/U3 session rail, the NOTIF-3 tray, and VDOCK-5's clock had ALREADY been consolidated into one full-width bottom rail (`notification_rail_with_sources`) by prior NAVBAR/NOTIF/CONSOLE-1 work, contradicting the doc comments that still described the clock as living in the left dock — the design doc's "top-bar+left-sidebar" framing inherited that staleness rather than dock.rs's real current state. So this slice's actual delta, verified against the code (not the doc): (1) reordered the auto-hide pin — previously wedged between the tray and the clock — to trail AFTER the clock, so the rail reads exactly "Start · sessions · tray · clock" contiguously (the pin isn't a Win7 taskbar concept, it's this shell's own dock-hold-open control); (2) relabelled the touch-density rail text "Advanced" → "Start" (label only, `dock.rs:1652` — the Start cell's click behavior is unchanged, still opens Console; WIN7-2 is the real two-pane Start Menu, not this unit); (3) applied lock #12's density pass — `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_H` (the default `Density::Mouse` rail) trimmed 20px → 18px via `Style::SP_M + Style::SP_XS / 2.0` (the same token-composition idiom `SYS_QUAD_ICON` already uses, not a new one-off constant), while `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_EXPANDED_H` (`Density::Touch`, 48px) is deliberately untouched since touch density exists specifically for bigger targets; (4) fixed the 4 stale doc comments (module header, VDOCK-1 banner, `dock()`, `paint_dock_frame()`) that mis-described clock/pin as left-dock content. Two new tests lock the contract end to end — `win7_1_the_taskbar_reads_start_sessions_tray_clock_left_to_right` (the full order incl. a real session entry) and `win7_1_the_default_taskbar_is_denser_than_the_shells_other_carbon_chrome` (pins `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_H` at 18px, denser than `DOCK_W`/`APP_CELL_H`, `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_EXPANDED_H` unchanged) — plus `navbar4_status_tray_is_folded_into_the_bottom_rail` extended with a pin-position assertion; all 54 `mde-shell-egui` `dock` tests pass on farm host 172.20.0.90 (`xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock`, 3m38s build, 0 failures) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean. In the rail's default (no status-panel) state `area_top == rail_rect.top()` exactly, so that subtraction was always `0.0`, pinning the WHOLE taskbar — paint and click hit-rects alike — at literal screen y=0. `git blame` + `git show 41314016` trace this to `e0b368e6` ("shell: move status into bottom rail", 2026-07-08, the commit that FIRST wrote `notification_rail_with_sources`) — two days BEFORE WIN7-1 (`41314016`, 2026-07-10), whose own diff never touches this code (verified: it only reorders the pin past the clock and trims a density constant) — so WIN7-1 INHERITED this bug rather than introducing it. **BROWSER-DD-0: BUGFIX — the browser never rendered any page (`run_tab` socket-protocol stub).** **As** a user, **I want** pages to actually render instead of hanging on "Loading the page…", **so that** the browser works at all. **Root cause:** `mde-web-preview`'s `tab` serve loop (`main.rs::run_tab`) was a STUB — it created the shm `FrameChannel`, `println!`'d `SHM_FD` to stdout, and looped `pump_step`+sleep with ZERO socket I/O, so it never sent `AttachFrame`/`PaintReady` over the session socket. The client (`WebSession`) only reaches `Live` (clears the "Loading…" note) on a `PaintReady` after an `AttachFrame` maps the shm fd, so EVERY url hung — not google-specific; the interactive browser had never worked. **Fix (landed):** (1) `run_tab` now speaks the BOOKMARKS-6 protocol in the confined child — wraps the session socket (the shell hands it as stdin/fd 0), sends `EventMsg::AttachFrame` + the shm fd via `SCM_RIGHTS`, then loops applying `ControlMsg` nav (Load/Reload/Back/Forward → the engine's methods; Resize/Input/verdict decoded-but-not-yet-wired), pumping a step, and signalling `PaintReady{seq}` on any delivered frame; a ~750ms first-frame watchdog force-emits so a slow/heavy SPA never hangs the shell — delivery is keyed on a delivered frame, NEVER on `LoadStatus::Complete`. (2) new `Engine::force_emit` (the watchdog path: paint+present+read-back+emit without a fresh frame-ready). (3) client Layer-A shm fallback in `WebSession::poll` — promotes to Live straight from the seqlock sequence, self-healing a `PaintReady` that arrived before `AttachFrame`. **Protocol sharing:** the excluded helper can't depend on the client, so it `#[path]`-includes the client's ONE `wire.rs` (single byte-exact source) + a minimal local `sock.rs` (SCM mechanism only); pinned by golden byte-compat tests on both ends. Tests: client Layer-A + early-PaintReady self-heal + golden (green, 40/40); helper wire golden + `sock` round-trips; `render-once` prints `FRAME_OK … distinct= mean_luma=` + `FORCE_OK` (force_emit live-exercise, separate process since Servo is one-instance-per-process). *(the release/RPM story unchanged: [[rpm-cut-needs-servo-build]] — the excluded helper still builds via `--manifest-path`)*

title: **BROWSER-DD-2: tabs + omnibox + compat chrome.** **As** a user, **I want** normal browsing, **so that** it's usable. **Acceptance:** standard tabs (open/close/switch/reorder) with a vertical-tabs option; unified URL+search omnibox with live SearXNG suggestions; back/fwd/reload/stop; HTTPS-only-with-prompt; block-3rd-party-cookies + clear-on-close; the Quasar-dashboard new-tab (SearXNG + mesh services); tested reachable. **Partial 2026-07-07:** the Browser surface now has a first-class compact tab strip over the existing isolated `WebSession` tab model: state chips + title/URL label, active switching, close behavior that drops/reaps the helper session, and a `+` new-tab intent drained by the live-helper shell arm into a real helper spawn. **Omnibox slice 2026-07-07:** toolbar Go and menu "Open Typed Address" now share one pure resolver: explicit schemes pass through (`https://`, `about:`, `data:`), likely hosts/IPs/host:port drafts become `https://…`, and free text searches the mesh-local SearXNG endpoint `https://search.mesh/search?q=…` with percent-encoded query text. **Tab-management slice 2026-07-07:** the tab strip now supports closing the last tab back to the honest gated/empty state and reordering tabs left/right while preserving the active `WebSession` identity. **Vertical-tabs slice 2026-07-07:** View → Vertical Tabs toggles a left-side tab rail backed by the same `WebSession` vector and the same switch/close/reorder/new-tab seams; no second tab model or fake helper path. **HTTPS-only prompt slice 2026-07-07:** explicit `http://` omnibox targets no longer load immediately; toolbar Go and menu Open Typed Address share one gate that shows an HTTP warning with Use HTTPS, Continue HTTP, and Cancel actions. Upgrading rewrites the target to `https://…`; continuing sends the original HTTP URL; hosts/searches keep the existing HTTPS-first resolver. **Quasar new-tab slice 2026-07-07:** `about:blank` now renders a native Quasar Browser dashboard over the real helper session with mesh SearXNG search plus service shortcuts for `search.mesh`, `music.mesh`, optional `horizon.mesh`, and `keystone.mesh`; dashboard searches load the SearXNG URL, and HTTP mesh services reuse the same HTTPS prompt gate. **Cookie/session policy slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser now exposes a Privacy menu that names the enforced engine policy (cookies blocked, third-party cookies blocked because no cookie store exists, session data cleared on tab close) plus a real Clear Current Tab Data action that resets the active helper session to the Quasar new-tab surface. Engine-side hardening remains structural: `secure_preferences()` disables the Servo cookie store, IndexedDB, StorageManager API, HTTP disk cache, WebRTC, and WebGPU, while the sandbox keeps persistence on tmpfs/no-home. **Live suggestions slice 2026-07-07:** the omnibox now fetches real mesh SearXNG suggestions from `https://search.mesh/autocompleter?q=...` on a short-lived off-thread reqwest client, only for plain search drafts (not URLs/hosts), renders the returned suggestions under the address bar, and routes accepted suggestions through the same omnibox resolver/load/HTTPS prompt path as Enter/Go. The parser accepts SearXNG/OpenSearch-style JSON arrays and object suggestion shapes, dedupes, and keeps tests offline. **Stop/compact Chromium chrome slice 2026-07-07:** added a real `ControlMsg::Stop` wire variant (tag `8`) plus `WebSession::stop()`. CEF tabs now turn the compact reload slot into a Chromium-style `×` Stop button while the active CEF session reports `loading`; the CEF helper dispatches that control to the pinned `cef_browser_t::stop_load` slot (`offset=112`, verified from the CEF 149 farm headers). Servo decodes the frame for protocol compatibility but does not show a fake stop action because the current Servo wrapper exposes no real cancel-load hook. Farm verification: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client -- --nocapture` passed **43/43** including `stop_drives_a_real_control_frame_over_the_seam`; `.50` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **57** unit tests plus bridge/protocol tests including `stop_control_uses_cef_stop_load_slot` and `ControlMsg::Stop` golden bytes; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **59/59**; `.50` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **26** unit tests plus headless-frame and protocol-golden tests; `git diff --check` passed. **Stop-button RPM + non-Eagle bench promotion 2026-07-07:** rebuilt `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` on BigBoy after the stop/compact chrome slice; RPM metadata reports build time `Tue Jul 7 21:41:57 2026`, payload size `286104730`, file size `112643918`, and SHA-256 `defb01d677ac56af2fff312e43d17984a2fb19cd9d13e3b66cd3a46ca641a734`. Payload inspection proves the browser helpers and CEF runtime assets still ship (`/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`, `mde-cef-runtime-setup.service`, `cef-linux64-minimal.env`). Non-Eagle farm gates passed on the candidate: L1 clean install **6/6**, L2 mini-mesh **15/15**, L3 stability/fd budget **14/14**, and L4 staged lighthouse replacement **33/33**. Eagle was excluded from this bench path per the operator directive, and no encrypted bench seat reboot was issued. **Stop-button Servo investigation 2026-07-10:** confirmed the "Servo exposes no real cancel-load hook" claim through direct source inspection of the pinned crates.io publications (not assumption) — downloaded and read `servo` 0.3.0 (`webview.rs`'s complete `impl WebView`: `load`/`load_request`/`reload`/`go_back`/`go_forward`/`notify_scroll_event`/`set_page_zoom`/etc, ~40 methods total, no `stop`; `servo.rs`'s complete `impl Servo`: `delegate`/`set_delegate`/`spin_event_loop`/`execute_webdriver_command`/`set_preference`/etc, no `stop`), `servo-embedder-traits` 0.3.0 (`webdriver.rs`'s `WebDriverCommandMsg` — the type `Servo::execute_webdriver_command` accepts — 24 variants, only `LoadUrl`/`Refresh`/`GoBack`/`GoForward` touch navigation, none cancel one; `WebDriverLoadStatus::NavigationStop` is a status the constellation reports back, not a sendable command), and `servo-constellation-traits` 0.3.0 (`EmbedderToConstellationMessage` — the internal channel `WebView::load`/`reload` send on — 32 variants, no stop/cancel-navigation variant either, and moot regardless because `WebView::inner()`/`inner_mut()`, the accessors that reach `constellation_proxy()`, are private and unreachable from outside the crate). Servo's own "Building a browser using Servo" tutorial (servo.org, 2024-09-11) documents no stop/cancel-load capability either. **Verdict: a genuine upstream Servo limitation in the pinned 0.3.0 release, confirmed at every reachable API layer — not a gap in this workspace's wrapper — so no fake/partial stop was implemented.** Left the honest no-fake-stop behavior in place: `mde-web-preview/src/main.rs`'s `apply_control` still decodes-and-intentionally-ignores `ControlMsg::Stop`, now with the full citation inline and split out into its own match arm, distinguished from the genuinely-"not yet" `Resize`/`Input`/`ResourceVerdict`/`CosmeticFilters`/`SavePdf` arms so the two different kinds of gap are not conflated. Extracted the shell's inline `can_stop` boolean into a named, doc-cited `can_show_stop_control(has_tab, crashed, loading, engine)` in `mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` (`nav_chrome`'s only caller) so a future refactor cannot silently start painting a fake, do-nothing Stop button for a Servo tab; behavior is unchanged (CEF-only). New tests (`mde-shell-egui` `web::tests`): `a_loading_cef_tab_shows_a_real_stop_control`, `a_loading_servo_tab_never_shows_a_fake_stop_control`, `stop_control_still_requires_a_live_loading_uncrashed_tab`. Farm verification: `.130` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client -- --nocapture` passed **51/51** including the pre-existing `stop_drives_a_real_control_frame_over_the_seam` (the shell-to-wire Stop send stays engine-agnostic and correct); `.130` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **177/177** including the three new `can_show_stop_control` tests, zero new warnings; `.50` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **39** lib unit tests plus **2** headless-frame and **2** protocol-golden tests (**43/43** total, exit code 0, `Finished` test profile in 11m01s — a cold Servo+SpiderMonkey rebuild on the 2-vCPU `.50` node); `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` clean on both touched files.

**BROWSER-DD-3: native ad/tracker blocking + privacy hardening.** **Acceptance:** uBlock-class native blocker on mesh-synced subscribable filter lists + custom rules + per-site toggle; anti-fingerprinting; per-site permission prompts + manager (default-deny); mesh-hosted safe-browsing blocklists; per-site data manager + forget; zero telemetry. **Native blocker policy slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser now starts every tab with the bundled EasyList/EasyPrivacy/uBlock seed filter store instead of an empty matcher, compiles that store into each `WebSession`, and re-applies it to all open tabs when policy changes. The Privacy menu now exposes the enforced cookie/no-store policy, the filter-list source, current first-party site, and a real per-site toggle: "Disable Blocking for This Site" writes a `FilterListStore` allowlist entry for the current host and changes subsequent helper `ResourceVerdict` frames from block to allow; "Enable Blocking for This Site" writes the re-block tombstone and restores block verdicts. Added a custom-rule injection seam (`FilterListSource::custom`) so synced/operator EasyList-format rules compile into active tab verdicts; `mde-web-preview-client` re-exports the store/source/host helpers so the shell and client use the same adblock model. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **62/62**, including `per_site_privacy_toggle_changes_real_resource_verdicts`, `custom_filter_rules_compile_into_open_tabs`, and Privacy menu model regressions; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client -- --nocapture` passed **43/43**; `git diff --check` clean. **Mesh allowlist action slice 2026-07-07:** the same per-site toggle now also publishes the existing mackesd adfilter worker verbs (`action/adfilter/allow` / `action/adfilter/block`) with the typed `{"domain":...}` body the worker drains into the mesh-synced LWW allowlist. The local Browser still applies the decision immediately, while the Bus action lets the `adfilter` worker replicate the site toggle across peers through the existing Syncthing store. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **63/63**, including `adfilter_domain_body_matches_the_worker_allow_block_shape`. **CEF anti-fingerprinting/zero-telemetry slice 2026-07-07:** the Chromium helper now pins a generic browser identity in both `cef_settings_t` and early Chromium argv (`user_agent`, `locale`, `accept_language_list`, `--user-agent`, `--lang`, `--accept-lang`) and starts CEF with privacy switches disabling background networking, sync, metrics/reporting, extension/device discovery surfaces, notifications/speech, WebRTC local-IP leakage, and Chromium ad/privacy-sandbox feature families including `PrivacySandboxAdsAPIs`, `InterestCohort`, `WebGPU`, `WebUSB`, and `WebBluetooth`. This brings the CEF path up to the existing Servo generic/no-persistence hardening baseline without changing the offscreen render seam. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **59** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests, including `init_paths_emit_cef_privacy_switches` and `owned_settings_pin_generic_browser_identity`. **Per-site permission manager slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser Privacy menu now exposes the enforced default-deny permission class list (`location, camera, microphone, notifications, clipboard`), shows the current first-party site's effective permission state, and provides a real "Forget Site Permissions" action that records a current-site permission reset without pretending unsupported allow toggles exist. This reflects the helpers' live deny-all prompt behavior (Servo denies `PermissionRequest`; CEF privacy switches disable the corresponding prompt/device surfaces) and keeps allow workflows gated until engine-level grants are wired. Farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web::menubar::tests::the_privacy_menu_exposes_the_enforced_cookie_policy -- --nocapture` passed, and `.90` broad `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **63/63**.

title: **BROWSER-DD-4: Widevine DRM.** **Acceptance:** the Widevine CDM fetched at first run (Firefox-style, not redistributed); DRM streaming (Netflix/Spotify web) plays on the CEF engine; the browser is fully functional (non-DRM) without it (honest gate). **First-run CDM provisioning/honest gate slice 2026-07-07:** the CEF helper now detects optional Widevine separately from the required CEF runtime (`WIDEVINE_OK` / `WIDEVINE_MISSING`) and keeps `probe` success tied only to CEF availability, so non-DRM browsing remains functional without a CDM. `CefLaunchPlan` carries `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_ROOT` and `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_LIB` only when `libwidevinecdm.so` is present, preserving an explicit renderer handoff for the later playback hook. Packaging now ships an operator-pinned first-run installer (`install-widevine-cdm`) plus `mde-widevine-cdm-setup.service`, rank-gated to Workstation and excluded from the server RPM; the manifest intentionally carries empty URL/SHA fields so Magic Mesh does not redistribute the CDM and exits with a clean `78` configuration gate until a licensed source is supplied. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **62** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests, including optional CDM detection and installer-manifest regressions; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**, including Workstation-only Widevine assets and Lighthouse mask coverage. **CEF native bridge Widevine handoff slice 2026-07-15:** `mde-web-cef-renderer` now consumes the helper-provided `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_ROOT` / `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_LIB` contract, fails closed when only one side is present, rejects missing roots/libs, rejects a CDM library outside the declared root, requires the library name `libwidevinecdm.so`, rejects sandbox-replaced/private roots (`/tmp`, home, root, var, run, and `/mnt`) before CEF starts, emits `WIDEVINE_BRIDGE_READY root=... lib=...` when valid, and exposes only the validated CDM root as an extra read-only bind inside the CEF OS sandbox. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed; `.90` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml --bin mde-web-cef-renderer -- --nocapture` passed **10/10** renderer contract/sandbox tests; `.130` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **150** lib tests plus **10** renderer tests, **2** bridge-handoff tests, protocol golden, and doc tests; `.130` `cargo build --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml --bins` passed; live `.50` fake-CDM regression reached `CEF_LAUNCH ... widevine=/tmp/mde-widevine-fake/libwidevinecdm.so`, then exited `78` with `CEF_BRIDGE_CONTRACT_INVALID reason=Widevine root /tmp/mde-widevine-fake is under /tmp`, proving bad CDM roots fail before Chromium subprocess startup. **Remaining:** supply a licensed CDM URL/SHA, install a real CDM on a CEF seat, and live-test Netflix/Spotify DRM playback; if playback still does not activate with the actual bundle, inspect the pinned CEF 149 Linux startup/hint-file path against that CDM.

**BROWSER-DD-5: WebExtensions (curated allowlist) + LastPass — deferred for v1 by BROWSER-CHROME E3.** **V1 decision 2026-07-14:** production Browser operation no longer depends on WebExtensions or LastPass autofill. The supported v1 path is native adblock, passkeys, reader mode, userscripts, and site styles; the curated registry and smoke fixture remain lab/future probes behind `MDE_CEF_WEBEXTENSIONS_LAB`. **Original future acceptance:** the CEF engine runs a VETTED allowlist of WebExtensions (LastPass, uBlock, password managers) from a mesh-hosted extension registry (sideload in Power mode); standard Chrome permission model; LastPass installs + autofills. *(CEF extension support is partial — validate/host-shim as needed)* **CEF WebExtensions registry gate slice 2026-07-09:** `mde-web-cef` now has a concrete mesh-hosted curated registry gate at `MDE_CEF_EXTENSION_REGISTRY` / `/mnt/mesh-storage/browser/extensions/allowlist.env`. The parser accepts only vetted `[extension.<chrome-id>]` sections, validates 32-character Chrome ids, safe names/versions/permission tokens, safe paths under the registry directory, and real unpacked extension directories; it reports `CEF_EXTENSIONS_READY` for a valid registry, `CEF_EXTENSIONS_MISSING` / `CEF_EXTENSIONS_INVALID` for honest gates, and now reports `CEF_EXTENSIONS_UNPROVEN` until live extension runtime smoke proves LastPass/uBlock behavior. A packaged operator template records the LastPass (`hdokiejnpimakedhajhdlcegeplioahd`) and uBlock Origin (`cjpalhdlnbpafiamejdnhcphjbkeiagm`) allowlist shape without bundling or pretending either extension runs yet. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-ext-cef-final` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml webextension -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.130` slot `browser-ext-cef-full-final` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **77/77** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests; `.170` slot `browser-ext-cef-fmt3` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed. Local `git diff --check` passed. A broad `cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings` probe remains blocked by pre-existing CEF helper lint debt outside this slice. **CEF WebExtensions launch-contract slice 2026-07-09:** a valid curated registry now affects the real CEF process contract instead of only status output. `CefLaunchPlan` carries vetted unpacked extension directories into `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_EXTENSIONS`, the native renderer validates every directory before initialization, and the CEF argv builder switches from `--disable-extensions` to an exact `--load-extension=<allowlist dirs>` plus `--disable-component-extensions-with-background-pages` only when registry validation succeeded. The bundled library now comes from a structured 100-rule Rust registry generated into the helper script, covering host-specific cleanup/readability fixes across video/music, news, docs/developer references, social feeds, commerce, recipes, and learning sites while preserving explicit cleanup on disable. **TRANSFERS-1: the `transfers` mackesd worker — queue, ledger, verbs.** **As** the platform, **I want** a daemon-owned transfer queue, **so that** jobs survive shell restarts and run on headless nodes. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): a rank-appropriate `transfers` worker (census entry per the BUG-STORAGE-1 lesson) owning a typed `TransferJob { id, source, dest, method, policy(bwlimit?/verify?), state }` envelope + a **persistent ledger** (queued/running/done/failed, resume state) on the node store; typed Bus verbs `transfer.submit(job)` / `.cancel(id)` / `.pause(id)` / `.resume(id)` / `.list` (Q14) with **CLI parity** (`mackesd transfer …`); parallel execution to a configurable cap (Q12); tested: submit→list round-trip, the state machine, cap enforcement, ledger persistence across a simulated restart. *(done: 6d5f5fb — workers/transfers/{job,ledger,queue,lane,verb}: TransferJob envelope (6 methods, 5-state machine, honest progress/error), LaneRunner async seam (GatedLaneRunner fails-honest until the lanes), TransferQueue (claim_next = the sole cap gate, orphan Running→Queued recovery), TransferVerb inbox (submit/cancel/pause/resume/list, daemon = single writer) + mackesd transfer CLI; census ("transfers",1) rank-Workstation. 42 tests. Lanes 2..6 implement LaneRunner.)*

**TRANSFERS-2: the http/wget lane + resume + throttle.** **As** a user, **I want** URL downloads, **so that** I can pull any file. **Acceptance:** an http lane shelling `wget -c --limit-rate` via the bounded-proc path (Q7); progress parsed from wget output (coarse fallback, never fake %); `wget -c` resume on `transfer.resume` (Q11); the per-job bandwidth limit honored (Q12); tested against a fixture http server: download→interrupt→resume continues (not restarts) + a bwlimit caps throughput. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Http` to `HttpWgetLane` and leaves non-HTTP lanes honestly gated; HTTP jobs run bounded `wget --continue --limit-rate=<policy>`, validate URL/destination/rate tokens, create destination parents, and keep child processes kill-on-drop for pause/cancel aborts. `ProgressSink` now lets lanes publish real parsed wget percentages into the queue ledger while Running, capped at 99 so terminal completion remains the only writer of 100. Farm-verified `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd transfers` = 29 passed, incl. fixture HTTP partial-file resume with a captured `Range: bytes=4096-`, wget progress-token parser tests, a measured 32 KiB fixture download capped by `--limit-rate=8k`, and queue progress persistence.)*

**TRANSFERS-3: the sftp lane (foreign hosts) + credential hygiene.** **As** a user, **I want** sftp put/get to any host, **so that** I reach off-mesh boxes. **Acceptance:** an sftp lane over the bounded-proc path to an arbitrary host:port (Q5); credentials entered per-job, **never written to the journal** (the Eagle sudo-in-journal lesson); progress + verify state; tested put+get against a fixture sshd + a credential-never-logged assertion. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Sftp` to a bounded OpenSSH `sftp` batch-mode lane. The lane supports one local endpoint plus one remote endpoint in either `sftp://user@host:port/path` or `user@host:path` form, so both put and get ride the same queue/ledger/progress/verify spine. It invokes `sftp` with `BatchMode=yes`, `StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new`, a generated batch file, and no password in argv; password-bearing URLs are rejected up front with a credential-hygiene error that does not echo the secret. SFTP percent tokens are parsed when the client emits them, without fabricated progress. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mackesd 'sftp_' -- --nocapture` = 5 passed/0 failed, including put+get through a fixture SFTP binary and password-URL rejection; `.90` focused real-fixture `cargo test -p mackesd sftp_lane_put_and_get_roundtrip_against_fixture_sshd -- --nocapture` = 1 passed/0 failed against farm `sshd`/`sftp`/`ssh-keygen`; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 58 passed/0 failed; `.170` `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*

**TRANSFERS-4: the rsync lane + recurring sync pairs.** **As** a user, **I want** delta mirrors + saved recurring syncs, **so that** big/repeat moves are efficient. **Acceptance:** an rsync lane (`rsync --partial --bwlimit`) with delta transfer + resume (Q7/Q11/Q12); a saved **sync pair** (schedule + source/dest) driven by a mackesd timer fires on schedule and mirrors (Q19); tested: a one-shot rsync round-trips + a recurring pair fires + mirrors on its tick (fixture clock). *(done 2026-07-07: the production `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Rsync` to `RsyncLane`; one-shot jobs run bounded `rsync --archive --partial --info=progress2`, validate source/dest/bwlimit tokens, create local destination parents when safe, parse real `progress2` percentages into the existing `ProgressSink`, and preserve kill-on-drop + timeout behavior. Added durable `sync-pairs/` records with typed save/remove verbs; the worker scans due pairs each tick and submits ordinary rsync jobs so recurring mirrors reuse the queue cap, ledger, progress, verify, and notification paths. Farm-verified on `172.20.0.50`: `timeout 180 cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 43 passed, including rsync argv/bwlimit validation, progress parser coverage, a real local one-shot fixture round-trip, sync-pair persistence/schedule coverage, and a recurring pair that fires and mirrors on tick.)*

Queue verification now resolves node destinations against the same staged mesh path, so a verified node job records the staged arrival without process-global env mutation in tests. Farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mackesd 'node_' -- --nocapture` = 101 passed/0 failed with the node cases selected; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 54 passed/0 failed; `.170` fresh `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*

**TRANSFERS-11: optional per-job integrity verify.** **As** a careful user, **I want** to verify a moved file, **so that** silent corruption is caught. **Acceptance:** a per-job "verify integrity" toggle (Q15) doing size + checksum on completion with a visible verified✓/mismatch✗ state; off by default; a mismatch marks the job failed (not silent); tested: a matching hash → verified, a corrupted fixture → failed. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferJob` now persists `integrity: Option<IntegrityStatus>` with durable `verified { size_bytes, sha256 }` and terminal `mismatch { source_size?, dest_size?, source_sha256?, dest_sha256?, error }` states. `TransferQueue::complete` honors `policy.verify`: local filesystem source/destination pairs are fingerprinted with size + SHA-256 after the lane reports `Done`; a match transitions to `Done` with progress `100`, while a mismatch transitions to `Failed` with the mismatch report and an honest error. Verification remains off by default. URL/remote sources fail honestly when verification is requested until those lanes can provide an expected source fingerprint, avoiding fake "verified" badges for HTTP downloads. Farm-verified `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd transfers` = 32 passed, including matching-local verified, corrupted-destination failed, unsupported-remote-source failed, and ledger/state-machine regressions.)*

Single terminal jobs emit a typed alert-shaped body with `source="transfers"`, severity `info` for Done and `warning` for Failed, plus transfer id/state/method. Multiple jobs reaped in the same scheduler pass coalesce into one summary alert, so a small batch does not create N toasts/messages. The notifier holds only the Bus root and opens `Persist` per emission to avoid carrying non-Send sqlite state across the async worker future. Farm-verified `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd transfers` = 34 passed, including failed-transfer alert emission and same-tick batch coalescing.)*

**NOTIF-2: the notify producer delta (mackesd, extend CHAT-FIX-2).** DONE 2026-07-07. Extended the existing `workers::notify` producer, not a new worker. It still owns severity tagging and the bounded coalescing ring, and now also publishes daemon-owned segment rollups on `state/notify/segment/{device,mesh,power,alerts}` for the shell pips. Source mapping: peers→Mesh, updates→Power, service/disk/SMART→Device, journal/cloud/node-grade→Alerts. The worker preserves the worst severity per segment so a lower warning cannot overwrite an active critical. It now watches the existing `<workgroup_root>/node-grade/*.json` mirror and emits D/F transitions (`D` warning, `F` critical), folds the existing OpenStack `event/notify/cloud` lane into the Alerts rollup without duplicating its Chat event, and uses affected-host attribution for critical policy: local criticals publish `own-seat-light-show`, remote/cloud/node-grade criticals publish `remote-pip-chat`. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/notify.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::notify --features async-services` (15 passed, including per-source fixtures, coalescing, node-grade D/F, cloud lane fold, worst-severity rollup, and cross-node policy). **Acceptance:** the existing notify worker tags each event Red/Amber/Blue (Q28), debounce+coalesces a flapping source into one updating item (Q32), publishes per-segment (Device/Mesh/Power/Alerts) worst-severity rollups the bar folds (Q47), watches the comprehensive source set (peers/failed-units/updates/disk-SMART/journal-WARN+/cloud/node-grade-D-F, Q33), and carries the cross-node critical policy (own-seat light-show vs remote pip+Chat, Q41); no new worker; fixture-tested per source → severity + one coalesced rollup.

**NOTIF-3: the 4-pip health bar + local-grade pip.** DONE 2026-07-07. The notification/status controls now mount in the full-width bottom rail, not the left rail: Advanced, Desktop, detected sessions, the Windows-style tray caret, Device/Mesh/Power/Alerts micro-icons, Pin, and Clock share the thin rail while the left dock keeps only app/workbench/system launch cells. `Shell::mount_dock_chrome` polls `StatusState` once per frame cadence and feeds the cloned daemon segment snapshot into `DockState`; missing rollups render with the dim baseline. The bottom-rail status cluster routes segment clicks through the dock (`Alerts -> Chat`, `Mesh -> MeshView`, Device/Power -> System), and the caret toggles the detail panel. The old `tray::status_quads` call is gone from `dock.rs` and its old mesh/seat/unread/session state is ignored at the dock boundary. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui rail --features drm` (14 passed, including Advanced/Start, Desktop split control, session rail, and source flyout regressions), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-theme tray_glyphs` (tray glyphs rasterize nonempty at 16/24 px), and full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm` (805 passed). **Acceptance:** the `status` module renders the Device·Mesh·Power·Alerts glyph+color pips in the bottom rail (Q5/Q9/Q11/Q13/Q24), each tinted by its segment rollup (Q21/Q22/Q23/Q11), healthy→faint dim baseline (Q15/Q45), **replacing BOTH VDOCK status quads** (Q6); segment click routes (Mesh→Mesh, Alerts→Chat, Device→sliders, Power→System, Q14); count-free (Q4/Q50); honest dim pre-poll; shell tests.

**NOTIF-4: the chevron expansion panel.** DONE 2026-07-07. The bottom-rail tray caret now toggles the detail panel from the Windows-style rail, preserving the panel after status icons moved out of the left dock. The panel uses the shared `Motion::BASE` animation value for both slide offset and opacity fade, renders the existing `status::status_panel` content above the rail, and expands the rail `Area` only while the panel is opening/open/closing so panel hit tests work without permanently widening chrome. It shows local + peer grades from `NodeGrades` (local first, peers worst-first through the status renderer) and the device band from the live `SeatSnapshot`; tapping a peer grade records the dock's Explorer node-focus request, and tapping the device band routes to System. Esc and click-away dismiss the panel. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock` (35 passed, including `the_status_chevron_opens_and_dismisses_the_detail_panel` exercising the rail caret and `the_status_panel_routes_device_controls_and_grade_rows`). **Acceptance:** a caret opens the slide/fade detail panel (Q17/Q27) showing local grade + peer grades (worst-first, tap→Explorer hero) + device sliders (Vol/BT, Q7/Q18); Esc/click-away dismiss; tested render + route.

**NOTIF-5: retire tray.rs into the one status module.** DONE 2026-07-07. Deleted `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/tray.rs` and removed `mod tray`; the retired 2×2 quad model, `TrayItem`, `TrayInputs`, `STATUS_QUADS`, `status_quads`, `quad_cell_id`, and tray-only tests are gone from live source. The surviving good folds live in the one status path: `status.rs` owns the battery fill ladder, system-pack pick, battery tone, segment pips, and NOTIF-4 device band; `chrome.rs` remains only the shared mesh-status/grade poll source consumed by dock status. Cleaned stale source comments that still referenced tray/status-quads. Evidence: `rg -n "TrayItem|TrayInputs|STATUS_QUADS|status_quads|quad_cell_id|tray::|crate::tray|mod tray|tray.rs" crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src` returns no matches; `rg -n "status quads|status-quad" crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/{main.rs,dock.rs,chrome.rs,chat.rs,curtain.rs}` returns no matches. **NOTIF-6: the critical edge-cue light-show.** DONE 2026-07-07. The shell now owns and mounts `status::CriticalEdgeCue` from the same daemon segment snapshot that feeds the dock pips. `Shell::render` updates it after the status poll with the local hostname, then draws the no-text all-edges cue after the curtain so an own-seat critical can still light the screen edges with the dock hidden/covered. The cue remains critical-only, own-seat-policy gated, acknowledges by edge click, clears on resolve, and is no longer hidden by DND/focus-mute because Critical alerts break through the platform suppression policy. Added a shell integration test proving a seeded own-seat critical registers the foreground edge-cue hit region from a normal shell frame. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui shell_mounts_the_critical_edge_cue_from_own_seat_rollups` (1 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status` (59 passed, including trigger/ack/resolve/Critical-breakthrough/pulse/tessellation tests), focused `win7_6_a_critical_firing_closes_an_open_start_menu` (1 passed), `win7_` (22 passed), and full BigBoy `mde-shell-egui` (1375 passed). **Acceptance:** a critical (red) fires an **all-edges Carbon-red pulsing light show** (Q34/Q37) that pulses a few cycles then settles to a held thin edge glow while live, clearing on resolve/ack (Q42); criticals only (warnings/info pull-only); fires on the affected node's OWN seat (Q41); not a text toast; tested trigger + settle + Critical breakthrough.

**NOTIF-7: OSD → centered Carbon pill; kill notification toasts.** DONE 2026-07-07. Restyled `mde-egui::ToastHost::osd` from the old bottom level bar into a centered Carbon pill with the hardware glyph, fill track, and percentage text. The shell `ToastBridge` no longer calls the chyron renderer or enqueues alert toasts; the `event/toast/show` lane still applies DND/focus/audio suppression and the single severity-scaled notification sound, while Chat remains the visible notification home. Packaging follow-up 2026-07-07: the full RPM now weak-recommends `libcanberra-gtk3`, which provides `canberra-gtk-play`, so fresh Workstations get the sound helper by default while the headless server variant stays seat-free and a minimal/opt-out install honestly degrades to silence. Added bridge tests proving an alert rings without tessellating popup geometry and that the OSD pill still renders through the shell bridge; added an RPM-manifest regression for the sound-player dependency. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-egui toast` (18 passed, including `osd_tessellates_a_real_centered_pill_when_present`), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui toast_bridge` (14 passed, including `an_alert_does_not_tessellate_a_notification_popup_through_the_bridge` and `the_osd_pill_still_tessellates_through_the_bridge`), full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-egui` (132 passed), full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (786 passed), and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services` (9 passed, including `base_rpm_recommends_the_notification_sound_player`). **Acceptance:** vol/brightness render a brief **centered Carbon pill** (glyph + fill bar + %, auto-dismiss, never hits Chat, Q16); the notification-class toast/chyron path is removed in favor of Chat (Q3); tested the pill renders + no notification toast appears.

**NOTIF-8: the Chat Notifications lane.** DONE 2026-07-06. Added the dedicated Chat **Notifications** lane in `chat.rs`: a session-only aggregate view over folded alert messages from the originating host contact, severity-colored and newest-first. The roster lane row and pane header show unread state while the shell Chat icon continues to count the underlying host alert once via the shared contact timeline. Opening the lane writes only the session watermark; source host attribution remains on every row. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests::notification` (3 passed) and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests` (24 passed). **Acceptance:** a dedicated Notifications lane in `chat.rs` (Q30), each item a message **from its originating host contact** (Q29), severity-colored newest-first, **session-only** (Q38), the **unread count on the Chat icon/lane header** (the only count, Q50); tested render + host-contact attribution + session-clear.

**NOTIF-9: typed configurable action buttons.** DONE 2026-07-07. Folded Chat alerts now carry a configurable typed action set (`AlertAction` / `AlertActionKind`: safe, destructive, ack, snooze) in addition to the legacy single `action_verb`; alert JSON can provide an `actions` array, while old `action` payloads still map to a safe Open action. The shell renders those typed actions in both the contact timeline and aggregate Notifications lane, posting `action/chat/alert-action` requests. The mackesd chat worker drains that verb: safe actions publish their configured Bus verb, destructive actions are refused until the UI sends an armed request, ack writes `state/chat/alert-ack/<message-id>`, and snooze writes `state/chat/alert-snooze/<message-id>`. The shell consumes those ack/snooze state topics so acknowledged/snoozed alerts leave the aggregate Notifications lane while remaining in append-only contact history; a newer live alert with a new id reappears. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/services/mde-chat/src/message.rs crates/services/mde-chat/src/alert.rs crates/services/mde-chat/src/lib.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/chat.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/chat.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-chat` (38 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::chat --features async-services` (32 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests` (26 passed). **Acceptance:** notifications carry a **configurable typed action set** (Q31/Q35) as Carbon buttons driving real mackesd verbs; destructive ones **typed-armed** in the message; ack clears + live re-raise + snooze (Q36); tested a safe action fires + an armed action gates + ack/re-raise.

**NOTIF-10: inline Chat notification config + DND.** DONE 2026-07-06. Added an aggregate Chat **Notifications** lane over folded host alert messages with inline controls for per-source mute, minimum alert severity, and the authoritative `dnd.yaml` toggle. Per-source mute persists through `NotifyPrefs`/`notify.json`; threshold changes publish `action/chat/notify-prefs` and persist through the chat worker; DND writes `mde_bus::dnd` while preserving topic snoozes. The feed remains populated even when muted/DND-gated; source-muted alerts are still logged. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-chat notify` (6 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::chat --features async-services` (31 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests` (23 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (785 passed). **Acceptance:** inline in the Chat lane (Q39): per-source mute + min-severity filter + a **DND** toggle that mutes the sliver+pulses but still fills the feed + keeps the Alerts pip live (Q40); persisted; tested each control applies.

**NOTIF-11: accesskit live-region for the pips/alerts.** DONE 2026-07-07. Enabled the shared `mde-egui` AccessKit feature for the shell and exported live accessibility nodes from the one status path: the whole 4-pip status strip now installs a polite `Role::Status` live region named "Notification status" with local grade plus Device/Mesh/Power/Alerts state; each segment pip installs a named `Role::Button` with its current state/action; and a live own-seat critical edge cue installs an assertive `Role::Alert` named "Critical alert". Farm evidence: `rustfmt --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/status.rs crates/shared/mde-egui/src/lib.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status::tests::status_bar_exports_accesskit_live_region_and_named_pips` (1 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status::tests::critical_edge_cue_exports_an_assertive_accesskit_alert` (1 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status` (35 passed). **Acceptance (operator a11y override, Q44):** the 4 pips + the Alerts state + a new critical are wired into accesskit as a live region a screen reader announces (name + state); tested the live-region nodes exist + update on state change.

**NOTIF-12: restyle the system quad to the pip language.** DONE 2026-07-07. The VDOCK-4 system quad now renders as compact glyph pips in the same language as the NOTIF status strip: inactive cells use the shared dim baseline, while hover/active states reveal semantic Carbon tones for Settings/info, Show-Desktop/success, Lock/warning, and Power/error. The routing/action seams are unchanged: Settings still opens System, Show-Desktop routes Desktop, Lock records the curtain-lock request, and Power opens the typed-armed power menu. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::system_quad_cells_use_status_pip_tint_language` (1 passed) and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock` (40 passed, including `each_system_quad_cell_dispatches_its_route_or_action`). **Acceptance:** the VDOCK-4 system quad (Settings/Show-Desktop/Lock/Power) adopts the same glyph+tint Carbon language as the pips (Q49), **actions unchanged**; tested render + actions still dispatch.

**NOTIF-13: surface-complete gate (Q48).** **DONE 2026-07-07.** Added the shell-level fixture `notif13_fixture_mounts_status_chat_edge_and_accesskit_together`, which mounts one real shell frame over a temp Bus fixture with daemon segment rollups + a folded critical Chat alert. The frame proves the bar, Alerts pip, chevron, expanded status panel, own-seat critical edge cue, Chat Notifications lane, typed Ack/Safe action buttons, DND toggle, and AccessKit polite/assertive live regions together. Added narrow test seams only: `ChatState::with_bus_root`, `ChatState::select_notifications_for_test`, `notification_count_for_test`, stable Chat action/DND response IDs, and `DockState::open_status_panel_for_test`. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --config skip_children=true --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/chat.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/main.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/dock.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui notif13_fixture_mounts_status_chat_edge_and_accesskit_together` (1 passed); `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (792 passed); `git diff --check` clean. No framebuffer capture utility was present on Eagle, so the pixel-level edge glow is proven by the fixture/accesskit/tessellation tests plus the live Bus+DRM scanout evidence rather than by a screenshot. **Acceptance:** an integration pass exercising the bar + expansion + edge-cue + Chat lane + actions + DND + accesskit together (fixtures); live-smoke on a seat that the calm bar renders + a fixture critical fires the edge-cue.

the tab strip, tab search, new-tab buttons, bookmarks row, find row, suggestions, compact tab menus, and Chrome toolbar menu use those Browser-local tokens instead of shared shell fills/text where they are part of first-party browser chrome. **Overlay token slice 2026-07-15:** Browser drawers and page-top permission/before-unload/password/offline/capture notices use Browser-local Material surface/text/status tokens;
